Infotexts.ntg.section.effects_withdrawal_ex1: Effects for Alcohol, Chemical and Opiate withdrawal
Infotexts.ntg.description.main_page: Main page for the Neurotrauma guide. \nThank you for installing my mod! I hope it helps you well. I'll actively be expanding it until all major NT addons have been integrated; though language localization is built-in I don't speak the major languages and thus cannot confirm whether translations would be great. \nIf you would want to help translate, there is a github page linked in the Mod's description; you can PR localization simply by translating the already existing localization files.
Infotexts.ntg.features.main_page: Page-linking: You can browse between all pages that matter just by clicking. Want to know the causes of a symptom? Just click! | Health Scanner / Hematology Analyzer integration: Neurotrauma changed afflictions that show up after scanning a patient can be clicked on and will open their respective page. | Search functionality: Built-in search functionality makes looking up things a breeze! | Index: Want to just browse pages? You can click on the button above and check out the pages, sorted by their category.
Infotexts.ntg.availableaddons.main_page: ‖color:35,38,115,255‖Neurotrauma‖color:end‖ by Guns | ‖color:84,84,97,255‖NT Cybernetics Enhanced‖color:end‖ by Nebual | ‖color:97,24,33,255‖NT Surgery Plus‖color:end‖ by Guns | ‖color:215,160,0,255‖NT Eyes‖color:end‖ by POSTACI, BETA and bludcart | ‖color:76,112,42,255‖NT Infections‖color:end‖ by guy | ‖color:165,22,166,255‖NT Grafting‖color:end‖ by Aegis_Myles | ‖color:0,175,175,255‖NT Lobotomy‖color:end‖ by Postaci | ‖color:3,99,138,255‖NT Thermal‖color:end‖ by BigEvilGreenBean | ‖color:20,60,0,255‖NT Airway Extension‖color:end‖ by moncka.ddima
Infotexts.ntg.description.vomiting_blood: Vomiting blood is a symptom, which means that it isn't fatal and doesn't cause any other afflictions.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.vomiting_blood: [internal_bleeding] Internal Bleeding (at 50% or more) | [liver_damage] Liver Damage (at 100%) | [anesthesia] Anesthesia
Infotexts.ntg.description.pale_skin: Pale skin is a symptom, which means that it isn't fatal and doesn't cause any other afflictions.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.pale_skin: [hypotension] Hypotension (at 50% or less) | [blood_loss] Blood Loss (at 40% or more)
Infotexts.ntg.description.shortness_of_breath: Shortness of breath is a symptom, which means that it isn't fatal and doesn't cause any other afflictions.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.shortness_of_breath: [heart_attack] Heart Attack | [lung_damage] Lung Damage (at 45% or more) | [pneumothorax] Pneumothorax (at 40% or more) | [cardiac_tamponade] Cardiac Tamponade (at 10% or more) | [hemotransfusion_shock] Hemotransfusion Shock (between 0 - 70%) | [hypoxemia] Hypoxemia (at 20% or more) | [heart_damage] Heart Damage (at 80% or more)
Infotexts.ntg.description.cough: Cough is a symptom, which means that it isn't fatal and doesn't cause any other afflictions.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.cough: [lung_damage] Lung Damage (at 50% or more) | [heart_damage] Heart Damage (at 50% or more) | [cardiac_tamponade] Cardiac Tamponade (at 20% or more)
Infotexts.ntg.description.nausea: Nausea is a symptom, which means that it isn't fatal and doesn't cause any other afflictions.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.nausea: [hemotransfusion_shock] Hemotransfusion Shock (between 0-90%) | [withdrawal] Withdrawal (at 40% or more) | [radiation_sickness] Radiation Sickness (at 80% or more) | [kidney_damage] Kidney Damage (at 60% or more) | [concussion] Concussion | Being near uncontained organs
Infotexts.ntg.description.increased_heartrate: Increased heartrate is a symptom, which means that it isn't fatal and doesn't cause any other afflictions; unless it is slowly increasing which would mean that you are undergoing fibrillation.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.increased_heartrate: [acidosis] Acidosis (at 20% or more) | [sepsis] Sepsis (at 20% or more) | [blood_loss] Blood Loss (at 40% or more) | [pneumothorax] Pneumothorax (at 30% or more) | [withdrawal] Withdrawal (Alcohol withdrawal, at 75% or more) | [mechanic_fibrillation] Fibrillation | [adrenaline] Adrenaline
Infotexts.ntg.description.confusion: Confusion is a symptom, which means that it isn't fatal and doesn't cause any other afflictions.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.confusion: [acidosis] Acidosis (at 15% or more) | [sepsis] Sepsis (at 40% or more) | [hypotension] Hypotension (at 30% or less) | [hypoxemia] Hypoxemia (at 50% or more) | [withdrawal] Withdrawal (Alcohol Withdrawal, at 80% or more) | [concussion] Concussion | [anesthesia] Anesthesia
Infotexts.ntg.description.lightheadedness: Lightheadedness is a symptom, which means that it isn't fatal and doesn't cause any other afflictions.
Infotexts.ntg.description.blurred_vision: Blurred vision is a symptom, which means that it isn't fatal and doesn't cause any other afflictions.
Infotexts.ntg.description.leg_swelling: Leg swelling is a symptom, which means that it isn't fatal and doesn't cause any other afflictions.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.leg_swelling: [liver_damage] Liver Damage (at 40% or more) | [kidney_damage] Kidney Damage (at 60% or more) | [heart_damage] Heart Damage (at 80% or more)
Infotexts.ntg.description.wheezing: Wheezing is a symptom, which means that it isn't fatal and doesn't cause any other afflictions.
Infotexts.ntg.description.weakness: Weakness is a symptom, which means that it isn't fatal and doesn't cause any other afflictions.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.weakness: [cardiac_tamponade] Cardiac Tamponade (at 30% or more) | [blood_loss] Blood Loss (at 60% or more) | [acidosis] Acidosis (at 35% or more)
Infotexts.ntg.effects.weakness: Decreases a character's melee damage by 80%
Infotexts.ntg.description.fever: Fever is a symptom, which means that it isn't fatal and doesn't cause any other afflictions.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.fever: [sepsis] Sepsis (5% or more) | [withdrawal] Withdrawal (Alcohol Withdrawal, at 90% or more) | [anesthesia] Anesthesia
Infotexts.ntg.description.sweating: Sweating is a symptom, which means that it isn't fatal and doesn't cause any other afflictions.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.sweating: [heart_attack] Heart Attack | [withdrawal] Withdrawal (Any type, at 30% or more)
Infotexts.ntg.description.bloating: Bloating is a symptom, which means that it isn't fatal and doesn't cause any other afflictions.
Infotexts.ntg.description.inflammation: Inflammation is a symptom, which means that it isn't fatal and doesn't cause any other afflictions.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.inflammation: [infected_wounds] Infected Wounds (at 10% or more) | [foreign_bodies] Foreign Bodies (at 15% or more)
Infotexts.ntg.description.jaundice: Jaundice is a symptom, which means that it isn't fatal and doesn't cause any other afflictions.
Infotexts.ntg.description.abdominal_discomfort: Abdominal discomfort is a symptom, which means that it isn't fatal and doesn't cause any other afflictions.
Infotexts.ntg.description.headache: Headache is a symptom, which means that it isn't fatal and doesn't cause any other afflictions.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.headache: [blood_loss] Blood Loss (at 50% or more) | [acidosis] Acidosis (at 20% or more) | [hypoxemia] Hypoxemia (at 40% or more) | [hypotension] Hypotension (at 60% or less) | [stroke] Stroke | [withdrawal] Withdrawal (Alcohol Withdrawal, at 50% or more) | [fractures] Fractures (Skull Fracture) | [concussion] Concussion
Infotexts.ntg.effects.headache: The character's screen will have a 'fuzzy' overlay
Infotexts.ntg.description.craving: Craving is a symptom, which means that it isn't fatal and doesn't cause any other afflictions.
Infotexts.ntg.description.palpitations: Palpitations are a symptom, which means that it isn't fatal and doesn't cause any other afflictions.
Infotexts.ntg.description.spasms: Spasms are a symptom, which means that they aren't fatal and don't cause any other afflictions. Spasms will cause the patient to jerk and flop around, making it harder to keep a hold on them. Spasms do this by spawning small invisible explosions on the limb that do no damage besides knockback.
Infotexts.ntg.effects.spasms: Character will occasionally fall to the ground and jerk around
Infotexts.ntg.description.abdominal_pain: Abdominal pain is a symptom, which means that it isn't fatal and don't cause any other afflictions.
Infotexts.ntg.effects.abdominal_pain: The character will have their speed cut in half for 1 second every 3 to 6 seconds
Infotexts.ntg.description.chest_pain: Chest pain is a symptom, which means that it isn't fatal and don't cause any other afflictions.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.chest_pain: [hemotransfusion_shock] Hemotransfusion Shock (Between 0 - 60%) | [fractures] Fractures (Rib Fracture) | [aortic_rupture] Aortic Rupture
Infotexts.ntg.effects.chest_pain: The character will have their speed cut in half for 1 second every 3 to 6 seconds
Infotexts.ntg.description.intense_pain: Intense pain is a symptom, which means that it isn't fatal and doesn't cause any other afflictions.
Infotexts.ntg.effects.intense_pain: The character will have their speed cut in half for 1 second every 3 to 6 seconds
Infotexts.ntg.treatments.intense_pain: Treating the causes | Stabilizing the dislocation with a bandage, or the fracture with a plaster cast | [analgesia] Analgesia (Painkiller) | [anesthesia] Anesthesia (Painkiller)
Infotexts.ntg.description.vomiting: Vomiting usually isn't harmful, and can sometimes be beneficial due to it causing alkalosis. Vomiting will also slow your character down by 20%.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.vomiting: [hemotransfusion_shock] Hemotransfusion Shock (between 0 - 40%) | [withdrawal] Withdrawal (Alcohol Withdrawal, 60% or more) | [drunk] Drunk (at 100%) | [kidney_damage] Kidney Damage (60% or more) | [raptor_bane_extract] Raptor Bane Extract
Infotexts.ntg.effects.vomiting: The character will be slowed down by 20% | [alkalosis] Alkalosis
Infotexts.ntg.description.neurotrauma: Neurotrauma is one of the only things that can deal direct damage to your health. Neurotrauma is extremely dangerous and can build up very quickly, and upon reaching 200%, will instantly kill the player. \nThe brain will heal at a rate of 0.1% per second, assuming that the config is unchanged and nothing is damaging it. Additionally, skull fractures prevent the natural regeneration of neurotrauma. \nThe gain or loss rate of neurotrauma depends on the affliction strengths of all its causes combined.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.neurotrauma: [stroke] Stroke | [liver_damage] Liver Damage (at 80% or more, point at which it outpaces natural regeneration) | [kidney_damage] Kidney Damage (at 100% or more, point at which it outpaces natural regeneration) | [sepsis] Sepsis (at 25% or more, point at which it outpaces natural regeneration) | [hypoxemia] Hypoxemia (at 10% or more, point at which it outpaces natural regeneration) | [tourniquet] Using a Tourniquet on the head | [methamphetamine] Methamphetamine | [hyperzine] Hyperzine | [traumatic_shock] Traumatic Shock (equal to strength divided by 100 per second, 10% or more is the point at which it outpaces natural regeneration) | [cyanide_poisoning] Cyanide Poisoning (at 70% or more) | [gunshot_wound] Has a chance to be inflicted after a gunshot wound to the head
Infotexts.ntg.effects.neurotrauma: Direct damage to vitality | Instant death, if at 200% | [respiratory_arrest] Respiratory Arrest (at 100% or more) | [unconsciousness] Unconsciousness (at 100% or more)
Infotexts.ntg.description.stroke: Stroke must be treated as quickly as possible to reduce the damage done to the brain. Streptokinase must NOT be used on patients stroking as it will thin the blood, preventing clotting, damaging the brain further.
Infotexts.ntg.description.withdrawal: Withdrawal is experienced when a character has an addiction yet not taken any substances for that addiction recently.
Infotexts.ntg.effects.generalwithdrawal: [craving] Craving (at 20% or more) | [sweating] Sweating (at 30% or more) | 10% slower movement speed (at 20% or more) | 30% slower movement speed (at 40% or more) | 50% slower movement speed (at 80% or more) | [nausea] Nausea (at 40% or more)
Infotexts.ntg.effects.alcoholwithdrawal: [fever] Fever (at 90% or more) | [vomiting] Vomiting (at 60% or more) | [headache] Headache (at 50% or more) | [confusion] Confusion (at 80% or more) | [increased_heartrate] Increased Heartrate (at 75% or more) | [seizure] Seizure (at 50% or more) | [hypertension] Hypertension
Infotexts.ntg.treatments.withdrawal: [haloperidol] Haloperidol (Only for Alcohol Withdrawal) | [naloxone] Naloxone (Only for Opiate Withdrawal) | [ambubag] Using an Ambubag filled with paint (Only for Chemical Withdrawal) | Administering more of the substance the individual is in withdrawal from | Bed rest
Infotexts.ntg.description.opiate_overdose: Opiate overdose is rare, and only occurs from abusing opiates. However, if above the 50% threshold it is very deadly and requires emergency care.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.opiate_overdose: Taking too many of the following opiates: | [opium] Opium | [morphine] Morphine | [fentanyl] Fentanyl | [analgesic_tank] Analgesic Tank
Infotexts.ntg.effects.opiate_overdose: [respiratory_arrest] Respiratory Arrest (at 60% or more) | [hypoventilation] Hypoventilation (at 30% or more) | [seizure] Seizure | [unconsciousness] Unconsciousness (at 60% or more) | 50% slower movement speed (at 50% or more)
Infotexts.ntg.description.seizure: Seizures will cause you to ragdoll whenever they trigger.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.seizure: [stroke] Stroke | [acidosis] Acidosis | [alkalosis] Alkalosis | [withdrawal] Withdrawal (Alcohol Withdrawal, at 50% or more) | [opiate_overdose] Opiate Overdose (at 50% or more) | [anesthesia] Anesthesia
Infotexts.ntg.description.unconsciousness: Unconsciousness usually means that something very wrong has happened, and the patient will need treatment as soon as possible. \nUnconsciousness will hide mental symptoms, and will also function as a Surgery Table for surgery, meaning that you can safely do surgery without a table or Analgesia / Anesthesia as long as your patient is unconscious.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.unconsciousness: Having less than 0 vitality | [hypoxemia] Hypoxemia (at 80% or more) | [neurotrauma] Neurotrauma (at 100% or more) | [coma] Coma (at 15% or more) | [aortic_rupture] Aortic Rupture | [seizure] Seizure | [anesthesia] Anesthesia | [opiate_overdose] Opiate Overdose (at 60% or more) | [stasis_bag] Being in a Stasis Bag
Infotexts.ntg.effects.unconsciousness: Inability to move or speak | [oxygen_low] Oxygen Low (if caused by having less than 0 vitality) | Allows surgical tools to be used on the character | [traumatic shock] Prevents Traumatic Shock from surgery
Infotexts.ntg.description.coma: Coma often happens after suffocating, and usually isn't fatal with proper medical care. The recommended course of action is to strap an autopulse onto the patient if it is severe enough.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.coma: [stroke] Stroke | [cardiac_arrest] Cardiac Arrest | [acidosis] High levels of Acidosis (at 60% or more) | [morbusine_poisoning] Morbusine Poisoning (at 20% or more) | [naloxone] Failing the Naloxone skill-check
Infotexts.ntg.effects.coma: [cardiac_arrest] Cardiac Arrest (at 40% or more) | [unconsciousness] Unconsciousness (at 15% or more)
Infotexts.ntg.description.spinal_cord_injury: Spinal cord injuries will remove your ability to move or speak, which could get you killed indirectly. Spinal cord injuries must be treated after treating the neck fracture, otherwise it will simply come back.
Infotexts.ntg.effects.spinal_cord_injury: Inability to move or speak | [analgesia] Analgesia
Infotexts.ntg.treatments.spinal_cord_injury: Apply Spinal Cord Implants before applying Sutures during an Osteosynthesis Surgery | [spinal_cord_implants] Spinal Cord Implants | [osteosynthesis_surgery] Osteosynthesis Surgery
Infotexts.ntg.description.concussion: A concussion is caused by a severe attack to the head. It periodically brings nausea, blurred vision, headaches, and confusion. While a nuisance, concussions aren't lethal. As they are a type of Damage (the Affliction Type), they can be treated with any items that will directly treat damage.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.concussion: [internal_wounds] Internal Wounds | [open_wounds] Deep Tissue Injuries and Bite Wounds (Open Wounds)
Infotexts.ntg.description.drunk: Drinking alcohol will cause the character to become drunk, causing a variety of mostly negative effects, especially at high strength.
Infotexts.ntg.effects.drunk: Decreased movement speed (at 20% or more) | Screen distortion (at 20% or more) | [analgesia] Analgesia (at 20% or more, will give no more than 5%) | Alcohol Addiction (at 20% or more) | [liver_damage] Liver Damage (at 40% or more)
Infotexts.ntg.description.respiratory_arrest: Respiratory arrest will quickly build up Oxygen Low and suffocate the victim, and must be dealt with swiftly. Being near a Surgery Table / Hospital Bed or wearing an AutoPulse will prevent the negative effects of respiratory arrest from taking place.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.respiratory_arrest: [lung_damage] Lung Damage (at 100%) | [traumatic_shock] Traumatic Shock (at 30% or more) | [neurotrauma] Neurotrauma (at 100% or more) | [hypoxemia] Hypoxemia (at 70% or more) | [opiate_overdose] Opiate Overdose (at 60% or more) | [stasis_bag] Stasis Bag | [morbusine_poisoning] Morbusine Poisoning (at 50% or more)
Infotexts.ntg.treatments.respiratory_arrest: Treating the causes | [ambubag] Applying an Ambubag with an oxygenite tank within it | Being conscious
Infotexts.ntg.firstaid.respiratory_arrest: [surgery_table] Being near a Surgery Table | [autopulse] Applying an Autopulse
Infotexts.ntg.description.lung_damage: Lung damage is not lethal, as long as it is below 100%. The lungs will heal at a rate of 0.01% per second; assuming that the config is unchanged, nothing is damaging them and they're below 100% damage.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.lung_damage: [abx] Broad-Spectrum Antibiotics | [liquid_oxygenite] Liquid Oxygenite | [ambubag] Using an Ambubag containing welding/incendium fuel or paint | Smoking | [hemotransfusion_shock] Hemotransfusion Shock (between 0 to 70%) | [radiation_sickness] Radiation Sickness (at 28% or more) | [sepsis] Sepsis (at 3% or more) | [hypoxemia] Hypoxemia (at 4% or more) | [open_wounds] Blunt Force Trauma (Open Wound) | [internal_wounds] Gunshot Wounds (Internal Wounds)
Infotexts.ntg.effects.lung_damage: [cough] Cough (at 50% or more) | [shortness_of_breath] Shortness of Breath (at 45% or more) | [respiratory_arrest] Respiratory Arrest (at 100%)
Infotexts.ntg.description.pneumothorax: Pneumothorax will prevent Hypoxemia from decreasing in strength. While not lethal on its own, Pneumothorax can easily kill if paired with afflictions that cause Hypoxemia. Pneumothorax's negative effects can be temporarily negated by using a Needle
Infotexts.ntg.causes.pneumothorax: [fractures] Fractures (Rib Fractures, if left unbandaged) | Trauma (Gunshots, explosions, lacerations, bite wounds, blunt force trauma) | [needle] Application of a Needle
Infotexts.ntg.effects.pneumothorax: [shortness_of_breath] Shortness of Breath (at 40% or more) | [hyperventilation] Hyperventilation (at 15% or more) | [increased_heartrate] Increased Heartrate (at 30% or more) | [hypoxemia] Prevents Hypoxemia from decreasing in severity
Infotexts.ntg.treatments.pneumothorax: [drainage] Using a drainage during surgery after Skin Retractors
Infotexts.ntg.description.hyperventilation: Hyperventilation is normally harmless, and can usually be ignored. Hyperventilation and Hypoventilation will cancel each other out.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.hyperventilation: [hypotension] Hypotension (at 80% or less) | [hypoxemia] Hypoxemia (at 10% or more) | [pneumothorax] Pneumothorax (at 15% or more) | [sepsis] Sepsis (at 15% or more) | [adrenaline] Adrenaline
Infotexts.ntg.description.hypoventilation: Hypoventilation is normally harmless, and can usually be ignored. Hypoventilation and Hyperventilation will cancel each other out.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.hypoventilation: [analgesia] Analgesia (at 20% or more) | [anesthesia] Anesthesia (at 40% or more) | [opiate_overdose] Opiate Overdose (at 30% or more)
Infotexts.ntg.description.oxygen_low: Oxygen Low means lack of oxygen in the lungs; Hypoxemia a lack of oxygen in the blood. Oxygen low causes hypoxemia, which is the most common way to get Neurotrauma.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.oxygen_low: [respiratory_arrest] Respiratory Arrest | [unconsciousness] Unconsciousness (if caused by vitality) | Being in water or a room without a source of oxygen | Pomegrenade Extract | [body_bag] Being in a Body Bag | [morbusine_poisoning] Morbusine Poisoning (at 50% or more)
Infotexts.ntg.treatments.oxygen_low: [surgery_table] Being near a Surgery Table or Hospital Bed | CPR | [autopulse] Applying an AutoPulse | Breathing oxygenated air | [ambubag] Applying an Ambubag with Oxygen / Oxygenite tanks
Infotexts.ntg.description.cardiac_arrest: Cardiac arrest will cause blood pressure to plummet significantly and must be dealt with swiftly. Cardiac arrest does not heal on its own, so it must always be directly treated.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.cardiac_arrest: [heart_damage] Heart Damage (at 100%) | [traumatic_shock] Traumatic Shock (at 40% or more) | [coma] Coma (at 40% or more) | [hypoxemia] Hypoxemia (at 80% or more) | [irregular_heartbeat] Irregular Heartbeat (at 20% or more) | [mechanic_fibrillation] Fibrillation | [manual_defibrillator] Manual Defibrillator | [stasis_bag] Being in a Stasis Bag | [morbusine_poisoning] Morbusine Poisoning (at 50% or more)
Infotexts.ntg.effects.cardiac_arrest: [coma] Coma | [acidosis] Acidosis | [hypotension] Hypotension (Minimum Blood Pressure) | [hypoxemia] Hypoxemia
Infotexts.ntg.treatments.cardiac_arrest: Treating the causes | CPR | [autopulse] AutoPulse | [adrenaline] Adrenaline | [manual_defibrillator] Manual Defibrillator | [aed] Automated External Defibrillator (AED)
Infotexts.ntg.description.cardiac_tamponade: Cardiac tamponade is common during missions involving combat outside the submarine, such as abandoned outposts or wrecks. Anyone showing symptoms should immediately head back to the submarine to get treated.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.cardiac_tamponade: [open_wounds] Recieving open wounds to the Torso
Infotexts.ntg.effects.cardiac_tamponade: [hypotension] Hypotension | [weakness] Weakness (at 30% or more) | [cough] Cough (at 20% or more) | [shortness_of_breath] Shortness of Breath (at 10% or more)
Infotexts.ntg.firstaid.cardiac_tamponade: [saline] Saline | [ringers_solution] Ringer's Solution | [adrenaline] Adrenaline | [deusizine] Deusizine | [autopulse] AutoPulse | CPR
Infotexts.ntg.description.heart_attack: A heart attack must be treated as soon as possible to reduce the risk of heart failure.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.heart_attack: [hypertension] Hypertension (at 150% or more) | [antibiotic_glue] Failing the Antibiotic Glue skill check
Infotexts.ntg.effects.heart_attack: [sweating] Sweating | [shortness_of_breath] Shortness of Breath | [heart_damage] Heart Damage
Infotexts.ntg.description.heart_damage: Heart damage is not lethal, as long as it is below 100%. The heart will heal at a rate of 0.01% per second; assuming that the config is unchanged, nothing is damaging it and it's below 100% damage.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.heart_damage: [heart_attack] Heart Attack | [abx] Broad-Spectrum Antibiotics | [liquid_oxygenite] Liquid Oxygenite | [hemotransfusion_shock] Hemotransfusion Shock (between 0 to 70%) | [mannitol] Mannitol | [radiation_sickness] Radiation Sickness (at 29% or more) | [sepsis] Sepsis (at 3% or more) | [hypoxemia] Hypoxemia (at 4% or more) | [internal_wounds] Blunt Force Trauma | [open_wounds] Gunshot Wounds | [sufforin_poisoning] Sufforin Poisoning (at 75% or more)
Infotexts.ntg.effects.heart_damage: [cough] Cough (at 50% or more) | [shortness_of_breath] Shortness of Breath (at 80% or more) | [leg_swelling] Leg Swelling (at 80% or more) | [cardiac_arrest] Cardiac Arrest (at 100%)
Infotexts.ntg.description.irregular_heartbeat: Irregular heartbeat is when the heart is not beating at its normal rate and can lead to Cardiac Arrest
Infotexts.ntg.treatments.irregular_heartbeat: Treating the causes | CPR | [autopulse] AutoPulse | [manual_defibrillator] Manual Defibrillator | [aed] Automated External Defibrillator (AED)
Infotexts.ntg.description.mechanic_fibrillation: Fibrillation will start off as increased heartrate which will slowly increase. Once Increased Heartrate reaches 100%, the victim will enter fibrillation, and symptoms will start.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.mechanic_fibrillation: [aortic_rupture] Aortic Rupture | [acidosis] Acidosis | [hypotension] Hypotension | [hypoxemia] Hypoxemia | [anesthesia] Anesthesia
Infotexts.ntg.treatments.mechanic_fibrillation: Treating the causes | [adrenaline] Adrenaline (slows down fibrillation) | [manual_defibrillator] Manual Defibrillator | [aed] Automated External Defibrillator (AED)
Infotexts.ntg.description.aortic_rupture: Aortic ruptures are one of the most serious injuries one can experience. The victim of this requires immediate medical care, and will most likely lose a significant portion of their blood.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.aortic_rupture: [bleeding] Bleeding (Torso, 80% or more) | [foreign_bodies] Foreign Bodies (Torso, 20% or more)
Infotexts.ntg.effects.aortic_rupture: [blood_loss] 4% Blood Loss per second | [internal_bleeding] 8% Internal Bleeding per second | [unconsciousness] Unconsciousness | [mechanic_fibrillation] Fibrillation | [abdominal_pain] Abdominal Pain | [chest_pain] Chest Pain
Infotexts.ntg.treatments.aortic_rupture: [aortic_rupture_surgery] Aortic Rupture Surgery | [open_close_surgery] Open / Close Surgery
Infotexts.ntg.description.internal_bleeding: Internal bleeding will quickly drain the blood out of the patient, and is an emergency in most cases.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.internal_bleeding: [open_wounds] Open Wounds | [liver_damage] Liver Damage (at 100%) | [aortic_rupture] Aortic Rupture
Infotexts.ntg.effects.internal_bleeding: [blood_loss] Blood Loss | [vomiting_blood] Vomiting Blood (at 50% or more)
Infotexts.ntg.treatments.internal_bleeding: [gel_ice_pack] Gel Ice Pack | [open_close_surgery] Open / Close Surgery | [combat_stimulant] Combat Stimulant
Infotexts.ntg.description.radiation_sickness: Low levels of radiation sickness are easily treatable, but if radiation sickness gets high enough, it will quickly begin killing the organs. Lungs will take more damage from radiation than other organs. Radiation sickness will appear on the health scanner at 25% and above.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.radiation_sickness: [health_scanner] Health Scanner (0.5% per second to person getting scanned, 0.3% per second to person scanning, 1% if used as a treatment, as you would a bandage) | Radiotoxin | Nuclear Explosives | Being near a Volatile Fulgurium Rod
Infotexts.ntg.effects.radiation_sickness: [liver_damage] Liver Damage (at 29% or more) | [heart_damage] Heart Damage (at 29% or more) | [kidney_damage] Kidney Damage (at 33% or more) | [lung_damage] Lung Damage (at 28% or more) | [bone_damage] Bone Damage (at 31% or more) | [nausea] Nausea (at 80% or more) | [seizure] Seizure (at 50% or more)
Infotexts.ntg.description.kidney_damage: Kidney damage is usually survivable, as even if it has progressed to kidney failure, it will still kill extremely slowly. \nKidney damage will not heal below 50% damage if it passes the 50% threshold, as one of the kidneys has died. The kidneys will heal at a rate of 0.01% per second if one kidney is alive, and will heal at a rate of 0.02% per second if both kidneys are alive; assuming the config is unchanged.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.kidney_damage: [abx] Broad-Spectrum Antibiotics | [liquid_oxygenite] Liquid Oxygenite | [hemotransfusion_shock] Hemotransfusion Shock (between 0 to 70%) | [mannitol] Mannitol | [radiation_sickness] Radiation Sickness (at 33% or more) | [sepsis] Sepsis (at 6% or more) | [hypoxemia] Hypoxemia (at 8% or more) | [internal_wounds] Blunt Force Trauma | [open_wounds] Gunshot Wounds | [sufforin_poisoning] Sufforin Poisoning (at 75% or more)
Infotexts.ntg.effects.kidney_damage: [leg_swelling] Leg Swelling (at 60% or more) | [acidosis] Acidosis (at 80% or more) | [hypertension] Hypertension | [bone_damage] Bone Damage (at 72% or more) | [vomiting] Vomiting (at 60% or more) | [neurotrauma] Neurotrauma (neutralizes Neurotrauma regeneration at 100%) | [nausea] Nausea (at 60% or more)
Infotexts.ntg.description.liver_damage: Liver damage, if progressed to liver failure, is fatal and can cause massive blood loss in the form of Internal Bleeding. \nThe liver will heal at a rate of 0.01% per second; assuming that the config is unchanged, nothing is damaging it and it's below 100% damage.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.liver_damage: [abx] Broad-Spectrum Antibiotics | [liquid_oxygenite] Liquid Oxygenite | [hemotransfusion_shock] Hemotransfusion Shock (between 0 to 70%) | [radiation_sickness] Radiation Sickness (at 29% or more) | [sepsis] Sepsis (at 3% or more) | [hypoxemia] Hypoxemia (at 4% or more) | [drunk] Drunk (over 40%) | [sufforin_poisoning] Sufforin Poisoning (at 75% or more) | [internal_wounds] Blunt Force Trauma | [open_wounds] Gunshot Wounds
Infotexts.ntg.effects.liver_damage: [leg_swelling] Leg Swelling (at 40% or more) | [internal_bleeding] Internal Bleeding (at 100%) | [vomiting_blood] Vomiting Blood (at 100%) | [hypertension] Hypertension | [neurotrauma] Neurotrauma (at 80% or more) | [abdominal_discomfort] Abdominal Discomfort (at 65% or more) | [jaundice] Jaundice (at 80% or more) | [bloating] Bloating (at 50% or more) | Reduces bleeding healing rate, bleeding will not heal at all at 100% Liver Damage
Infotexts.ntg.description.morbusine_poisoning: An affliction showing the presence of a lethal poison; fatal if left untreated.
Infotexts.ntg.effects.morbusine_poisoning: Screen Distortions | Slower Movement Speed | Chromatic Abberation | [oxygen_low] Oxygen Low (between 10 to 40%) | [respiratory_arrest] Respiratory Arrest (at 40% or more) | [cardiac_arrest] Cardiac Arrest (at 60% or more) | Stun (various vitality thresholds)
Infotexts.ntg.treatments.morbusine_poisoning: Morbusine Antidote | [toxin_filter] Having Toxin Filter in a Surgery Table and laying down
Infotexts.ntg.description.cyanide_poisoning: An affliction showing the presence of a lethal poison; fatal if left untreated.
Infotexts.ntg.effects.cyanide_poisoning: Screen Distortions | Screen Blurring | Skin turns blue | Reduced Movement Speed | Reduces Husk Infection (at 10% or more) | [hypoxemia] Hypoxemia (at 10% or more) | Stun (various vitality thresholds)
Infotexts.ntg.treatments.cyanide_poisoning: Cyanide Antidote | [toxin_filter] Having Toxin Filter in a Surgery Table and laying down
Infotexts.ntg.description.sufforin_poisoning: An affliction showing the presence of a lethal poison; fatal if left untreated.
Infotexts.ntg.effects.sufforin_poisoning: Screen Distortions | Screen Blurring | Reduces Husk Infection (at 10% or more) | [liver_damage] Liver Damage (at 75% or more) | [heart_damage] Heart Damage (at 75% or more) | [kidney_damage] Kidney Damage (at 75% or more) | [vanilla_organ_damage] Vanilla Organ Damage (at 75% or more) | Stun (75% or above under 90% vitality)
Infotexts.ntg.treatments.sufforin_poisoning: Sufforin Antidote | [toxin_filter] Having Toxin Filter in a Surgery Table and laying down
Infotexts.ntg.description.vanilla_organ_damage: A side effect of some medicines. Additional organ damage is caused by failing the medical skill check of those medicines. \nIn Neurotrauma, it is not considered a form of Damage (the Affliction Type) and cannot be treated by sources which treat damage.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.vanilla_organ_damage: [alien_blood] Alien Blood (+3.75 or 7.5%) | [abx] Broad-Spectrum Antibiotics (+20 or 12% over antibiotics duration) | Chloral Hydrate (+2.5%) | [liquid_oxygenite] Liquid Oxygenite (+6 or 10%) | [mannitol] Mannitol (+5 or 10%) | [methamphetamine] Methamphetamine (+15 or 30%) | [sufforin_poisoning] Sufforin Poisoning (at 75% or more) | [internal_wounds] Blunt Force Trauma | [open_wounds] Gunshot Wounds
Infotexts.ntg.effects.vanilla_organ_damage: Direct Vitality damage based on affliction strength
Infotexts.ntg.treatments.vanilla_organ_damage: Time | [thiamine] Thiamine | [deusizine] Deusizine | [organ_transplant_surgery] Organ Transplant Surgery will heal some vanilla organ damage with fresh organs
Infotexts.ntg.description.analgesia: The first step in all surgical procedures. Analgesia is an affliction that prevents traumatic shock during surgery and relieves pain.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.analgesia: [opium] Opium | [morphine] Morphine | [fentanyl] Fentanyl | [analgesic_tank] Analgesic Tank | [adrenaline] Adrenaline (doesn't give more than 4.5%) | [spinal_cord_injury] Spinal Cord Injury | [drunk] Drunk (at 20% or more, doesn't give more than 4.5%) | [anesthesia] Anesthesia
Infotexts.ntg.effects.analgesia: [hypoventilation] Hypoventilation (at 20% or more) | 25% Damage resistance (at 20% or more) | 25% Stun resistance (at 20% or more) | Removes aim penalty from damaged arms (at 20% or more) | [internal_damage] Prevents Fractures/Dislocations from causing Internal Damage | [wrench] Reduces Wrench skill-check medical requirement | Enables surgery tool usage on the patient
Infotexts.ntg.description.anesthesia: Anesthesia is an affliction that prevents traumatic shock during surgery, and unlike Analgesia, doesn't require a Surgery Table to perform safe surgery.
Infotexts.ntg.effects.anesthesia: [hypoventilation] Hypoventilation (at 40% or more) | [unconsciousness] Unconsciousness (at 15% or more) | [analgesia] Analgesia (no more than 5%) | 50% slower movement speed
Infotexts.ntg.sideeffects.anesthesia: [hypotension] Hypotension | [confusion] Confusion | [blurred_vision] Blurred Vision | [fever] Fever | [mechanic_fibrillation] Fibrillation | [seizure] Seizure | [vomiting_blood] Vomiting Blood | Psychosis
Infotexts.ntg.description.arterial_bleeding: Arterial ruptures are extremely dangerous and require emergency care as soon as possible. Applying a Tourniquet to the afflicted limb can buy you time to treat it, but this should not be done on the head. \nThe arteries which may rupture include the carotid artery on the head, brachial arteries of the arms, and femoral arteries of the legs. Rupture of the torso artery is an Aortic Rupture.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.arterial_bleeding: [bleeding] Bleeding (at 80% or more) | [traumatic_amputation] Traumatic Amputation | [foreign_bodies] Foreign Bodies (at 20% or more) | Chance to happen when a bone is fractured
Infotexts.ntg.effects.arterial_bleeding: [blood_loss] 1.5% Blood Loss per second (doesn't give more than 99.5% blood loss)
Infotexts.ntg.description.traumatic_amputation: Traumatic amputation is the non-surgical amputation of an extremity. A traumatic amputation will act the exact same as a Surgical Amputation, besides the fact that you will acquire Arterial Bleeding upon first getting amputated. \nA limb must be fractured in order to become traumatically amputated.
Infotexts.ntg.effects.armtraumatic_amputation: 1 arm amputation will prevent 2-handed item usage | 2 arm amputations will prevent you from interacting with anything
Infotexts.ntg.effects.legtraumatic_amputation: 1 leg amputation will halve your speed | 2 leg amputations will ragdoll you and remove your ability to speak
Infotexts.ntg.treatments.traumatic_amputation: [amputation_surgery] Amputation Surgery (use limbs before sutures for reattachment)
Infotexts.ntg.description.surgical_amputation: Surgical amputation is the amputation of an extremity. Possible amputations include arms, legs, and the head. The head cannot be reattached after amputation. Unlike traumatic amputations, surgical amputations will not cause Arterial Bleeding upon first acquiring them.
Infotexts.ntg.effects.armsurgical_amputation: 1 arm amputation will prevent 2-handed item usage | 2 arm amputations will prevent you from interacting with anything
Infotexts.ntg.effects.legsurgical_amputation: 1 leg amputation will halve your speed | 2 leg amputations will ragdoll you and remove your ability to speak
Infotexts.ntg.description.gangrene: Gangrene is a rare occurrence, and only occurs due to neglecting an applied Tourniquet, Endovascular Balloon or Sepsis. Once gangrene hits 15%, it is irreversible and the limb must be amputated.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.gangrene: [tourniquet] Leaving a Tourniquet on for too long | [endovascular_balloon] Leaving an Endovascular Balloon in for too long | [sepsis] Sepsis (at 5% or more)
Infotexts.ntg.description.plaster_cast: Plaster casts are extremely important for treating limb Fractures. Assuming the config is unchanged, getting a fracture on a limb that is already casted will break the cast. Plaster casts can be removed with Trauma Shears.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.plaster_cast: [gypsum] Applying Gypsum to a limb that is Bandaged
Infotexts.ntg.effects.plaster_cast: [fractures] Slowly treats limb fractures by 0.33% per second | Reduces movement speed by 20% for each cast
Infotexts.ntg.description.clamped_arteries: Clamped Arteries are caused by using a Tourniquet on an extremity (except the head) with Arterial Bleeding or by using a hemostat.
Infotexts.ntg.effects.clamped_arteries: [arterial_bleeding] Prevents Arterial Bleeding from causing Blood Loss | [gangrene] Causes Gangrene after a period of time
Infotexts.ntg.description.bone_death: Bone death - while usually not lethal - can kill if you lack Osteosynthesis Implants.
Infotexts.ntg.description.bone_damage: Bone damage is not lethal, and can usually be ignored as long as it has not progressed to Bone Death
Infotexts.ntg.causes.bone_damage: [kidney_damage] Kidney Damage (at 72% or more) | [radiation_sickness] Radiation Sickness (at 31% or more) | [sepsis] Sepsis (at 50% or more) | [hypoxemia] Hypoxemia (neutralizes regeneration at 100%)
Infotexts.ntg.description.fractures: Fractures are common injuries, which most crewmembers will get often. Fractures will not kill, and should be treated last in an emergency. Having a fractured limb is required for it to become a Traumatic Amputation, except for that on the head.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.fractures: [internal_wounds] Internal Wounds | [open_wounds] Open Wounds | [bone_death] Bone Death | [dislocations] Failing the Wrench skill check while treating Dislocations
Infotexts.ntg.effects.legfractures: 1 leg fracture halves movement speed | 2 leg fractures force prone position | [intense_pain] Intense Pain | [internal_damage] Internal damage (if not bandaged/casted/sedated)
Infotexts.ntg.effects.armfractures: 1 arm fracture prevents 2-handed item usage | 2 arm fractures will prevent you from interacting with anything | [intense_pain] Intense Pain | [internal_damage] Internal damage (if not bandaged/casted/sedated)
Infotexts.ntg.effects.neckfractures: [spinal_cord_injury] Spinal Cord Injury | [internal_damage] Internal Damage (if not bandaged)
Infotexts.ntg.treatments.fractures: [plaster_cast] Plaster Cast (Arm/Leg only) | [osteosynthesis_surgery] Osteosynthetic Surgery (All fractures)
Infotexts.ntg.description.dislocations: Dislocations are common injuries, which most crewmembers will get often. Dislocations will not kill, and should be treated last in an emergency. A limb can be both dislocated and fractured.
Infotexts.ntg.effects.legdislocations: 1 leg dislocation halves speed | 2 leg dislocations forces crawling | [intense_pain] Intense Pain | [internal_damage] Internal Damage if not bandaged/sedated
Infotexts.ntg.effects.armdislocations: 1 arm dislocation prevents 2-handed item usage | 2 arm dislocations will prevent you from interacting with anything | [intense_pain] Intense Pain | [internal_damage] Internal Damage if not bandaged/sedated
Infotexts.ntg.treatments.dislocations: [wrench] Applying a wrench (requires 60 medical or only 30 with analgesia)
Infotexts.ntg.description.burns: First degree burns are minor and can usually be ignored, but second degree burns and higher require medical intervention, lest the patient risk contracting an infection. \nBurns between 1-20% strength are first degree, 21-50% are second degree, and 51% and onwards are third degree. The sum of acid burns and regular burns will be accounted in its degree. \nIt is important to note that unlike other traumatic afflictions which directly affect vitality, burns will never appear on the Health Scanner
Infotexts.ntg.effects.burns: Direct vitality damage | [bandages] Turns Bandages applied to the burned limbs dirty | [infected_wounds] Infected Wounds (depending upon burn severity)
Infotexts.ntg.treatments.burns: [bandages] Bandages (Only on first and second degree burns) | [plastiseal] Plastiseal (Only on first and second degree burns) | [open_close_surgery] Applying Plastiseal before Sutures during Open / Close Surgery | [antibiotic_ointment] Antibiotic Ointment (Only on first and second degree burns) | [antibiotic_glue] Antibiotic Glue (works on any degree burns) | [combat_stimulant] Combat Stimulant (works on any degree burns)
Infotexts.ntg.description.open_wounds: The Damage (Affliction Type) that a person may receive is divided into two groups based on their properties, Open Wounds and Internal Wounds. All open wounds will cause Infected Wounds proportional to their severity, and may be treated with Sutures. They will also typically - though not always - be caused by sources which also inflict Bleeding. \nUnless specified, the below mentioned afflictions are gained based on chance. This means more damage taken will result in a higher chance of gaining them. The afflictions are gained on the limb in which damage was taken, so afflictions not applicable to the limb will not be gained.
Infotexts.ntg.description.open_wounds.lacerations: A wound typically resulting from injuries involving sharp objects.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.open_wounds.lacerations: Being hit by sharp weapons, such as a knife | Submarine spalling | [tweezers] Using Tweezers when removing Foreign Bodies or Internal Wounds
Infotexts.ntg.effects.open_wounds.lacerations: Direct Vitality loss proportional to the amount of damage taken | [infected_wounds] Infected Wounds over time, depending on severity | [fractures] Arm / Leg fractures (if more than 5 damage was taken) | [fractures] Skull fractures (if more than 15 damage was taken, 70% chance of occurring) | [fractures] Rib Fractures (if more than 10 damage was taken) | [pneumothorax] Pneumothorax (if more than 5 damage was taken) | [cardiac_tamponade] Cardiac Tamponade (if more than 5 damage was taken) | [internal_bleeding] Internal Bleeding (if more than 5 damage was taken; random range between 0.2 - 0.5 times the damage taken) | [traumatic_amputation] Traumatic Amputation (if more than 1 damage was taken on an already fractured limb)
Infotexts.ntg.treatments.open_wounds.lacerations: Time (unless over 50 strength) | [bandaged] Bandaging (unless over 50 strength) | [sutures] Sutures | Pomegrenade Extract | [combat_stimulant] Combat Stimulant
Infotexts.ntg.description.open_wounds.deeptissuein: A wound typically resulting from injuries involving explosions.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.open_wounds.deeptissueinjury: Being hit by explosives, such as a Frag Grenade
Infotexts.ntg.effects.open_wounds.deeptissueinjury: Direct Vitality loss proportional to the amount of damage taken | [infected_wounds] Infected Wounds over time, depending on severity | [fractures] Arm / Leg fractures (if more than 1 damage was taken) | [fractures] Skull fractures (if more than 15 damage was taken, 70% chance of occurring) | [fractures] Neck fractures (if more than 15 damage was taken) | [fractures] Rib Fractures (if more than 10 damage was taken) | [pneumothorax] Pneumothorax (if more than 5 damage was taken) | [cardiac_tamponade] Cardiac Tamponade (if more than 5 damage was taken) | [internal_bleeding] Internal Bleeding (if more than 5 damage was taken; proportional to a random range between 0.2 - 0.5 times the damage taken) | [traumatic_amputation] Traumatic Amputation (if more than 1 damage was taken on an already fractured limb) | [foreign_bodies] Foreign Bodies (75% of occurring, proportional to half the damage taken) | [concussion] Concussion (if more than 15 damage was taken, 70% chance of occurring) | [dislocations] Dislocations (if more than 1 damage was taken, 35% chance of occurring)
Infotexts.ntg.treatments.open_wounds.deeptissueinj: Time (unless over 50 strength) | [bandaged] Bandaging (unless over 50 strength) | [sutures] Sutures | Pomegrenade Extract | [combat_stimulant] Combat Stimulant
Infotexts.ntg.description.open_wounds.gunshotwound: A wound typically resulting from injuries involving firearms.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.open_wounds.gunshotwound: Being shot with bullet-firing weapons, such as a revolver
Infotexts.ntg.effects.open_wounds.gunshotwound: Direct Vitality loss proportional to the amount of damage taken | [infected_wounds] Infected Wounds over time, depending on severity | [fractures] Arm / Leg fractures (if more than 1 damage was taken) | [fractures] Skull fractures (if more than 15 damage was taken, at least a 70% chance of occurring) | [fractures] Rib Fractures (if more than 1 damage was taken, no more than 30% chance of occurring) | [lung_damage] Lung Damage (if more than 1 damage was taken, proportional to damage taken) | [heart_damage] Heart Damage (if more than 1 damage was taken, proportional to damage taken) | [kidney_damage] Kidney Damage (if more than 2 damage was taken, proportional to damage taken) | [liver_damage] Liver Damage (if more than 2 damage was taken, proportional to damage taken) | [vanilla_organ_damage] Vanilla Organ Damage (if more than 1 damage was taken; proportional to a fourth of damage taken) | [pneumothorax] Pneumothorax (if more than 1 damage was taken) | [cardiac_tamponade] Cardiac Tamponade (if more than 5 damage was taken) | [internal_bleeding] Internal Bleeding (if more than 5 damage was take; proportional to a random range between 0.2 - 0.6 times the damage taken) | [traumatic_amputation] Traumatic Amputation (if more than 1 damage was taken on an already fractured limb) | [foreign_bodies] Foreign Bodies (up to 30 strength proportional to damage taken when a fracture occurs, or at a 75% chance up to 20 strength proportional to a fourth of damage taken) | [neurotrauma] Neurotrauma (if more than 5 damage was taken; has a 70% chance of occurring proportional to 0.1 - 0.4 times the damage taken)
Infotexts.ntg.treatments.open_wounds.gunshotwound: Time (unless over 50 strength) | [bandaged] Bandaging (unless over 50 strength) | [sutures] Sutures | Pomegrenade Extract | [combat_stimulant] Combat Stimulant
Infotexts.ntg.description.open_wounds.bitewound: A wound typically resulting from injuries involving creature attacks.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.open_wounds.bitewound: Being hit by a creature, such as a Crawler | Being bit by a human husk's ovipositor (stinger)
Infotexts.ntg.effects.open_wounds.bitewound: Direct Vitality loss proportional to the amount of damage taken | [infected_wounds] Infected Wounds over time, depending on severity | [fractures] Arm / Leg fractures (if more than 5 damage was taken) | [fractures] Skull fractures (if more than 15 damage was taken, 70% chance of occurring) | [fractures] Rib Fractures (if more than 10 damage was taken) | [pneumothorax] Pneumothorax (if more than 5 damage was taken) | [internal_bleeding] Internal Bleeding (if more than 5 damage was taken; proportional to 0.2 - 0.5 times the damage taken) | [traumatic_amputation] Traumatic Amputation (if more than 5 damage was taken on an already fractured limb) | [concussion] Concussion (if more than 15 damage was taken; has at least a 70% of occurring)
Infotexts.ntg.treatments.open_wounds.bitewound: Time (unless over 100 strength) | [bandaged] Bandaging (unless over 100 strength) | [sutures] Sutures | Pomegrenade Extract | [combat_stimulant] Combat Stimulant
Infotexts.ntg.description.internal_wounds: The Damage (Affliction Type) that a person may receive is divided into two groups based on their properties, Open Wounds and Internal Wounds. All internal wounds do not cause Infected Wounds. \nUnless specified, the below mentioned afflictions are gained based on chance. This means more damage taken will result in a higher chance of gaining them. The afflictions are gained on the limb in which damage was taken, so afflictions not applicable to the limb will not be gained.
Infotexts.ntg.description.internal_wounds.bft: This type of wound is typically not associated with bleeding, but may still cause life-threatening afflictions if it is a strong hit. Does not naturally regenerate past 100 strength.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.internal_wounds.bft: Falling | Being knocked into a solid object | Being crushed by a submarine | Being hit by blunt weapons, such as a crowbar
Infotexts.ntg.effects.internal_wounds.bft: Direct Vitality loss proportional to the damage taken | [dislocations] Dislocations (if more than 2 damage was taken) | [fractures] Arm / Leg fractures (if more than 2 damage was taken) | [fractures] Skull fractures (if more than 15 damage was taken, 70% of occurring) | [fractures] Neck fractures (if more than 15 damage was taken) | [fractures] Rib Fractures | [pneumothorax] Pneumothorax | [vanilla_organ_damage] Vanilla Organ Damage OR Neurotrauma organ damage (Random range between 0 and the amount of damage taken) | [traumatic_amputation] Traumatic Amputation (if more than 15 damage was taken and a fracture is already present on the limb) | [concussion] Concussion (if more than 15 damage was taken, 70% of occurring)
Infotexts.ntg.treatments.internal_wounds.bft: Time (unless over 100 strength) | [bandaged] Bandaging (unless over 100 strength) | [gel_ice_pack] Gel Ice Pack (unless over 100 strength) | [open_close_surgery] Applying Tweezers before Sutures during Open / Close Surgery | [combat_stimulant] Combat Stimulant | Pomegrenade Extract
Infotexts.ntg.description.internal_wounds.internal: An uncommon wound which is typically caused by other afflictions and failed skill checks.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.internal_wounds.internaldamag: [dislocations] Dislocations without a bandage | [fractures] Fractures without a plaster cast | [tweezers] Failing the skill check for Tweezers | [sutures] Failing the skill check for Sutures | [skin_retractors] Failing the skill check for Skin Retractors | [surgical_drill] Failing the skill check for the Surgical Drill | [surgical_saw] Failing the skill check for the Surgical Saw | [spinal_cord_implants] Failing the skill check for Spinal Cord Implants | [osteosynthesis_implants] Failing the skill check for Osteosynthesis Implants
Infotexts.ntg.effects.internal_wounds.internaldama: Direct Vitality loss proportional to the damage taken | [dislocations] Dislocations | [fractures] Arm / Leg fractures (after taking at least 5 damage) | [fractures] Skull fractures (after taking at least 15 damage; has at least a 70% chance of occuring) | [fractures] Neck fractures (after taking at least 15 damage) | [fractures] Rib Fractures (after taking at least 5 damage) | [pneumothorax] Pneumothorax (after taking at least 5 damage) | [vanilla_organ_damage] Vanilla Organ Damage OR Neurotrauma organ damage (random range between 0 and the damage taken) | [traumatic_amputation] Traumatic Amputation (after taking at least 10 damage on a fractured limb) | [concussion] Concussion (after taking at least 15 damage; has at least a 70% chance of occuring)
Infotexts.ntg.treatments.internal_wounds.internald: Time | [open_close_surgery] Applying Tweezers before Sutures during Open / Close Surgery | [combat_stimulant] Combat Stimulant | Pomegrenade Extract
Infotexts.ntg.description.infected_wounds: Infected wounds are caused by letting wounds fester; they should be cleaned in order to prevent Sepsis.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.infected_wounds: [open_wounds] Open Wounds left untreated | [burns] Burns | Having a dirty bandage on the limb will dramatically increase infection speed
Infotexts.ntg.effects.infected_wounds: [sepsis] Sepsis (at 50% or more) | [inflammation] Inflammation (at 10% or more) | Decreased Immunity
Infotexts.ntg.treatments.infected_wounds: [antibiotic_ointment] Antibiotic Ointment | [antibiotic_glue] Antibiotic Glue | [antibiotic_sprayer] Antiseptic Sprayer
Infotexts.ntg.description.foreign_bodies:
Foreign bodies are often acquired after firefights or being caught in an explosion. Foreign bodies should be removed as quickly as possible in order to avoid Arterial Bleeding or the patient contracting Sepsis. 
Each use of tweezers will remove 3-10% foreign bodies. 

 The chances of Foreign Bodies causing issues scale according to time and strength; the longer they are in or the stronger the affliction is, the higher the chance.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.foreign_bodies: Explosions | Gunshots (If a gunshot fractures a bone it will always cause Foreign Bodies)
Infotexts.ntg.effects.foreign_bodies: [arterial_bleeding] Arterial Bleeding (at 20% or more) | [aortic_rupture] Aortic Rupture (at 20% or more, if on Torso) | [sepsis] Sepsis (at 20% or more) | [inflammation] Inflammation (at 15% or more)
Infotexts.ntg.treatments.foreign_bodies: [open_close_surgery] Applying Tweezers before Sutures during Open / Close Surgery | [tweezers] Using Tweezers directly on a wound (causes Traumatic Shock!)
Infotexts.ntg.description.bleeding: Bleeding will cause the patient to gain Blood Loss based on the intensity and duration of the bleeding.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.bleeding: [open_wounds] Lacerations or Bite Wounds | Failing certain skill checks
Infotexts.ntg.effects.bleeding: [arterial_bleeding] Arterial Bleeding (at 80% or more) | [aortic_rupture] Aortic Rupture (at 80% or more, if on Torso) | [blood_loss] Blood Loss
Infotexts.ntg.description.ointmented: Being ointmented will help protect Wounds from infection and is very useful for patients suffering from Burns (which may infect quickly!).
Infotexts.ntg.effects.ointmented: [infected_wounds] -1.5% Infected Wounds per second (if below 60% strength)
Infotexts.ntg.description.chilled: The affliction given by the gel ice pack when used. Greatly increases natural regeneration of blunt force trauma, though will worsen infections if present. It will also increase the regeneration of Internal Bleeding.
Infotexts.ntg.effects.chilled: Slows character by 5% per chilled limb | [internal_wounds] Increases natural healing of Blunt Force Trauma | [infected_wounds] Makes Infected Wounds worsen faster | [internal_bleeding] Decreases Internal Bleeding by 0.2% every second
Infotexts.ntg.description.bandaged:
The Bandaged affliction is the result of applying Bandages / Plastiseal. 

Bandages are an extremely versatile first aid item and it is therefor advised that every crewmember keeps atleast some bandages on them at all times to use in emergencies. 
Bandages will slowly turn dirty over time - though this process will happen faster if the wound is bleeding. The Bandaged affliction can be removed with Trauma Shears or a Diving Knife.
Infotexts.ntg.effects.bandaged: [dislocations] Prevents Internal Damage or Intense Pain from Dislocations| [fractures] Prevents Internal Damage or Intense Pain from Fractures | [fractures] Prevents Rib Fractures from causing Pneumothorax | [dislocations] Removes slow down penalty from Dislocations | Slows you down by 10% for each bandaged limb | [open_wounds] Accelerates natural healing of Open Wounds | [burns] Accelerates natural healing of Burns | [infected_wounds] Protects against Infected Wounds
Infotexts.ntg.description.skin_ointmented: Improves natural healing rate of Burns, Open Wounds, and Blunt Force Trauma. Unlike being Bandaged or Ointmented, Skin Ointment has no additional effect.
Infotexts.ntg.effects.skin_ointmented: [burns] Decreases Burns by 0.12 per second | [open_wounds] Decreases Open Wounds by 0.12 per second | [internal_wounds] Decreases Blunt Force Trauma by 0.12 per second
Infotexts.ntg.description.hypoxemia: Hypoxemia is the absence of adequate oxygen in the bloodstream. Hypoxemia can quickly build up and cause the patient to spiral out of control, therefor a patient suffering from this will need immediate medical care. A Pneumothorax will prevent Hypoxemia from going down, proportional to its strength.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.hypoxemia: [cardiac_arrest] Cardiac Arrest | [oxygen_low] Oxygen Low | [blood_loss] Blood Loss (at 40% or more) | [hypotension] Hypotension (at 70% or less) | [cyanide_poisoning] Cyanide Poisoning (at 10% or more)
Infotexts.ntg.effects.hypoxemia: [mechanic_fibrillation] Fibrillation | [liver_damage] Liver Damage (at 4% or more, point at which it outpaces natural regeneration) | [heart_damage] Heart Damage (at 4% or more, point at which it outpaces natural regeneration) | [lung_damage] Lung Damage (at 4% or more, point at which it outpaces natural regeneration) | [kidney_damage] Kidney Damage (at 8% or more, point at which it outpaces natural regeneration; changes to 4% if one kidney is already dead) | [hyperventilation] Hyperventilation (at 10% or more) | [neurotrauma] Neurotrauma (at 10% or more, point at which it outpaces natural regeneration) | [shortness_of_breath] Shortness of Breath (at 20% or more) | [headache] Headache (at 40% or more) | [confusion] Confusion (at 50% or more) | [respiratory_arrest] Respiratory Arrest (at 70% or more) | [cardiac_arrest] Cardiac Arrest (at 80% or more) | [unconsciousness] Unconsciousness (at 80% or more) | [bone_damage] Bone Damage (Will prevent regeneration of Bone Damage at 100%)
Infotexts.ntg.description.sepsis: Sepsis is a dangerous affliction caused by letting Infected Wounds fester; though it is rather easy to spot as (by default) only one other affliction in the game will cause a Fever
Infotexts.ntg.causes.sepsis: [azathioprine] Failing the Azathioprine skill check | [blood_packs] Blood Packs who's donor had Sepsis | [gangrene] Gangrene (at 15% or more) | [foreign_bodies] Foreign Bodies (at 20% or more) | [infected_wounds] Infected Wounds (at 50% or more) | [dialysys_filter] Dialysis Filter (0.1% chance per second when in use) | [toxin_filter] Toxin Filter (0.1% chance per second when in use)
Infotexts.ntg.effects.sepsis: [liver_damage] Liver Damage (at 3% or more, point at which it outpaces natural regeneration) | [heart_damage] Heart Damage (at 3% or more, point at which it outpaces natural regeneration) | [lung_damage] Lung Damage (at 3% or more, point at which it outpaces natural regeneration) | [kidney_damage] Kidney Damage (at 6% or more, point at which it outpaces natural regeneration; changes to 3% if one kidney is already dead) | [neurotrauma] Neurotrauma (at 25% or more, point at which it outpaces natural regeneration) | [hyperventilation] Hyperventilation (at 15% or more) | [gangrene] Gangrene (at 5% or more) | [fever] Fever (at 5% or more) | [increased_heartrate] Increased Heartrate (at 20% or more) | [confusion] Confusion (at 40% or more) | [bone_damage] Bone Damage (at 50% or more, point at which it outpaces natural regeneration)
Infotexts.ntg.description.hemotransfusion_shock: Hemotransfusion shock is a condition that only occurs when administering an incompatible blood type to someone (example: giving a O+ person A+ blood).
Infotexts.ntg.causes.hemotransfusion_shock: [blood_packs] Incompatible Blood Packs (gives 100% strength) | [alien_blood] Alien Blood (gives 99% strength over time)
Infotexts.ntg.effects.hemotransfusion_shock: [vomiting] Vomiting (between 0-40%) | [chest_pain] Chest Pain (between 0-60%) | [blood_loss] Blood Loss (between 0-70%) | [liver_damage] Liver Damage (between 0-70%) | [heart_damage] Heart Damage (between 0-70%) | [kidney_damage] Kidney Damage (between 0-70%) | [lung_damage] Lung Damage (between 0-70%) | [shortness_of_breath] Shortness of Breath (between 0-70%) | [abdominal_pain] Abdominal Pain (between 0-80%) | [wheezing] Wheezing (between 0-90%)
Infotexts.ntg.description.blood_loss: While blood loss can be extremely resource-intensive to cure if it's severe enough; though when it's at or below 30% it can usually be ignored.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.blood_loss: [internal_bleeding] Internal Bleeding | [empty_blood_pack] Empty Blood Pack uses | [bleeding] Bleeding | [arterial_bleeding] Arterial Bleeding (will not give more than 99.5% Blood Loss) | [aortic_rupture] Aortic Rupture | [surgery_incision] Surgery Incision (unless they have Clamped Bleeding) | [hematology_analyzer] Hematology Analyzer uses | [hemotransfusion_shock] Hemotransfusion Shock (between 0-70%)
Infotexts.ntg.effects.blood_loss: [hypotension] Hypotension (scales depending on the amount of Blood Loss) | [hypoxemia] Hypoxemia (at 40% or more) | [increased_heartrate] Increased Heartrate (at 40% or more) | [pale_skin] Pale Skin (at 40% or more) | [headache] Headache (at 50% or more) | [weakness] Weakness (at 60% or more)
Infotexts.ntg.treatments.blood_loss: Time | [blood_packs] Administering Blood Packs | [deusizine] Deusizine | [combat_stimulant] Combat Stimulant
Infotexts.ntg.description.hypotension: Hypotension is blood pressure that has dropped below normal levels. Hypotension is lethal if it is severe enough. Lower percentages correspond to lower blood pressure.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.hypotension: [blood_loss] Blood Loss (scales according to the amount of lost blood) | [traumatic_shock] Traumatic Shock | [anesthesia] Anesthesia | [irregular_heartbeat] Irregular Heartbeat | [sodium_nitroprusside] Sodium Nitroprusside (reduces Blood Pressure by 45%) | [nitroglycerin] Nitroglycerin (reduces Blood Pressure by 45%) | [cardiac_arrest] Cardiac Arrest (sets Blood Pressure to the minimum amount) | [cardiac_tamponade] Cardiac Tamponade (halves blood pressure)
Infotexts.ntg.effects.hypotension: [mechanic_fibrillation] Fibrillation | [confusion] Confusion (at 30% or less) | [pale_skin] Pale Skin (at 50% or less) | [blurred_vision] Blurred Vision (at 55% or less) | [headache] Headache (at 60% or less) | [lightheadedness] Lightheadedness (at 60% or less) | [hypoxemia] Hypoxemia (at 70% or less) | [hyperventilation] Hyperventilation (at 80% or less)
Infotexts.ntg.treatments.hypotension: Treating the causes | CPR | [blood_packs] Blood Packs | [saline] Saline | [ringers_solution] Ringer's Solution | [autopulse] AutoPulse | [adrenaline] Adrenaline | [deusizine] Deusizine
Infotexts.ntg.description.hypertension: Hypertension is blood pressure that has risen above normal levels. Hypertension may start becoming lethal if blood pressure is over 150%. Higher percentages correspond to higher blood pressure.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.hypertension: [kidney_damage] Kidney Damage | [liver_damage] Liver Damage | [blood_packs] Blood Packs | [saline] Saline | [ringers_solution] Ringer's Solution | [autopulse] AutoPulse | [adrenaline] Adrenaline | [deusizine] Deusizine | [withdrawal] Withdrawal (Alcohol) | CPR
Infotexts.ntg.effects.hypertension: [stroke] Stroke (at 150% or more) | [kidney_damage] Kidney Damage (at 184% or more, point at which it outpaces natural regeneration; changes to 152% if one kidney is already dead) | [heart_attack] Heart Attack (at 150% or more)
Infotexts.ntg.treatments.hypertension: Treating the causes | [blood_loss] Inducing Blood Loss | [sodium_nitroprusside] Sodium Nitroprusside | [nitroglycerin] Nitroglycerin
Infotexts.ntg.description.acidosis: Acidosis is usually ignorable, and minimal action is required at low to medium severity. Acidosis and Alkalosis will cancel each other out.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.acidosis: [hypoventilation] Hypoventilation | [respiratory_arrest] Respiratory Arrest | [cardiac_arrest] Cardiac Arrest | [kidney_damage] Kidney Damage (at 80% or more) | [blood_packs] Blood Packs who's donor had acidosis (Acidosis gained will be the donor's acidosis level at time of extraction divided by 5) | [saline] Saline
Infotexts.ntg.effects.acidosis: [seizure] Seizure (at 60% or more) | [coma] Coma (at 60% or more) | [increased_heartrate] Increased Heartrate (at 20% or more) | [mechanic_fibrillation] Fibrillation | [confusion] Confusion (at 15% or more) | [headache] Headache (at 20% or more) | [weakness] Weakness (at 35% or more)
Infotexts.ntg.treatments.acidosis: Treating the causes | [alkalosis] Inducing Alkalosis | [blood_packs] Transfusing neutral Blood Packs | [dialysis_filter] Having a Dialysis Filter in a Surgery Table and laying on it
Infotexts.ntg.description.alkalosis: Alkalosis is usually ignorable, and minimal action is required at low to medium severity. Alkalosis and Acidosis will cancel each other out.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.alkalosis: [hyperventilation] Hyperventilation | [vomiting] Vomiting | [blood_packs] Blood Packs who's donor had Alkalosis (Alkalosis gained will be the donor's acidosis level at time of extraction divided by 5)
Infotexts.ntg.effects.alkalosis: [seizure] Seizure (at 60% or more) | [palpitations] Palpitations (at 20% or more)
Infotexts.ntg.treatments.alkalosis: Treating the causes | [acidosis] Inducing Acidosis (preferably with Saline) | [blood_packs] Transfusing neutral Blood Packs | [dialysis_filter] Having a Dialysis Filter in a Surgery Table and laying on it
Infotexts.ntg.description.immunity: Immunity helps fight Infected Wounds and increases natural healing. If your immunity is 6% or lower, you can use incompatible blood types without triggering Hemotransfusion Shock. \nImmunity must be below 10% to safely transplant organs if you have organ rejection turned on in the config menu.
Infotexts.ntg.effects.immunity: [burns] Helps healing Burns | [open_wounds] Helps healing Open Wounds | [internal_wounds] Helps healing Internal Wounds
Infotexts.ntg.description.organ_rejection: This mechanic is off by default. It can be toggled in the mod's config, found in the ESC menu while the mod is enabled. \nOrgan rejection may occur when a patient receives an organ transplant while their Immunity is higher than 10%. If organ rejection occurs, the organ that was transplanted will immediately gain 100% damage, making it useless. \nReducing Immunity is primarily done using Azathioprine.
Infotexts.ntg.description.traumatic_shock: Traumatic shock can rapidly become fatal, and should your patient acquire this at any point, it is likely to be unintended. Traumatic shock will decrease in strength 4x as fast provided the victim is under the effects of Anesthesia, or has Analgesia while being near a Surgery Table. \nIf a character has Retracted Skin and the round ends (such as on docking to a station), the character will gain 5 minutes of traumatic shock immunity on the next round.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.traumatic_shock: [surgery_incision] Making Surgery Incision without Analgesia | [clamped_bleeding] Any Surgical steps after Clamped Bleeding without Anesthesia or Table | [tweezers] Using Tweezers on Gunshot Wounds or Deep Tissue Injuries (causes minor shock unless overused)
Infotexts.ntg.effects.traumatic_shock: [hypotension] Hypotension (a large amount) | [cardiac_arrest] Cardiac Arrest (at 40% or more) | [respiratory_arrest] Respiratory Arrest (at 30% or more) | [neurotrauma] Neurotrauma (equal to strength divided by 100 per second, 10% or more is the point at which it outpaces natural regeneration) | Psychosis (equal to strength divided by 100 per second)
Infotexts.ntg.treatments.traumatic_shock: [sutures] Ending the surgery immediately and waiting | [surgery_table] Being near a Surgery Table while sedated
Infotexts.ntg.prevention.traumatic_shock: [surgery_table] Use a Surgery Table | [analgesia] Sedate with Analgesia (requires a Surgery Table) | [anesthesia] Sedate with Anesthesia
Infotexts.ntg.description.surgery_incision: The second step in all surgical procedures. Having an unclamped (no Clamped Bleeding) surgical incision will cause 0.7% Blood Loss per second for each incision, and having a surgical incision without Analgesia or another sedation method (Unconsciousness or Anesthesia) will cause 2% Traumatic Shock per second for each incision.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.surgery_incision: [scalpel] Applying a Scalpel | [multipurpose_scalpel] Applying a Multipurpose Scalpel in 'Surgery Incision' mode
Infotexts.ntg.effects.surgery_incision: [blood_loss] Blood Loss (if no Clamped Bleeding) | [traumatic_shock] Traumatic Shock if unsedated / not near a Surgery Table
Infotexts.ntg.treatments.surgery_incision: [sutures] Use Sutures to end Surgery early | [hemostat] Use a Hemostat to continue Surgery
Infotexts.ntg.description.clamped_bleeding: The third step in most surgical procedures. Having clamped bleeding will prevent Blood Loss caused by Surgery Incision. This affliction is required for the next step, the Skin Retractors.
Infotexts.ntg.effects.clamped_bleeding: [blood_loss] Prevents Blood Loss from a Surgery Incision
Infotexts.ntg.treatments.clamped_bleeding: [sutures] Use Sutures to end Surgery early | [skin_retractors] Use Skin Retractors to continue Surgery
Infotexts.ntg.description.retracted_skin: The fourth step in most surgical procedures. Having Retracted Skin without a Surgery Table nearby will cause 2% Traumatic Shock per second for each instance of retracted skin. Most surgical procedures, such as Osteosynthetic Surgery or Organ Transplant Surgery branch off from this step.
Infotexts.ntg.effects.retracted_skin: [traumatic_shock] Traumatic Shock if unsedated / not near a Surgery Table
Infotexts.ntg.treatments.retracted_skin: [sutures] Use Sutures to end Surgery early | [surgical_drill] Use a Surgical Drill to continue Osteosynthetic Surgery | [multipurpose_scalpel] Use a Multipurpose Scalpel on Adaptive Organ Extraction mode to continue Organ Transplant Surgery | [surgical_saw] Use a Surgical Saw to continue Amputation Surgery
Infotexts.ntg.description.drilled_bones: The fifth step in Osteosynthetic Surgery. Having drilled bones without a Surgery Table nearby will cause 1.5% Traumatic Shock per second for each instance of drilled bones.
Infotexts.ntg.effects.drilled_bones: [traumatic_shock] Traumatic Shock if unsedated / not near a Surgery Table
Infotexts.ntg.treatments.drilled_bones: [sutures] Use Sutures to end Surgery early | [osteosynthesis_implants] Use Osteosynthesis Implants to continue Osteosynthetic Surgery
Infotexts.ntg.description.sawed_bones: The fifth step in Amputation Surgery. Having Sawed Bones without a Surgery Table nearby will cause 1.5% Traumatic Shock per second for each instance of Sawed Bones.
Infotexts.ntg.effects.sawed_bones: [traumatic_shock] Traumatic Shock if unsedated / not near a Surgery Table
Infotexts.ntg.treatments.sawed_bones: [sutures] Use Sutures to end Amputation Surgery | [arm_item] Use a severed / bionic Arm to attach before ending Amputation Surgery with Sutures | [leg_item] Use a severed / bionic Leg to attach before ending Amputation Surgery with Sutures
Infotexts.ntg.description.adrenaline: Adrenaline is used in the treatment of Cardiac Arrest. In return for curing the affliction, it raises the patient's blood pressure by 30%. Alongside these effects, it gives the patient Analgesia (but wont give more than 4.5%), Increased Heartrate, Hyperventilation, increases their melee damage by 20%. halves the gain of Fibrillation and decreases the potency of slowing effects.
Infotexts.ntg.seealso.adrenaline: [cardiac_arrest] Cardiac Arrest | [analgesia] Analgesia | [increased_heartrate] Increased Heartrate | [hyperventilation] Hyperventilation | [mechanic_fibrillation] Fibrillation
Infotexts.ntg.description.alien_blood: Medical skill check: 40. \n\nUsing Alien Blood in place of regular blood is extremely dangerous and should only be used in dire situations.
Infotexts.ntg.description.ambubag:
An item used to provide supplementary oxygen if a patient isn't breathing enough or isn't breathing at all. 
Using the ambubag with a regular oxygen tank in it will treat 90% Oxygen Low, and use 2% of the oxygen in the tank. 
Using the ambubag with an oxygenite tank in it will treat 90% Oxygen Low, cure Respiratory Arrest, and use 2% of the oxygen in the tank.

Using the ambubag with a welding fuel tank in it will cause 110% Oxygen Low, cause 10% Lung Damage, and use 5% of the welding fuel in the tank. 
Using the ambubag with a incendium fuel tank in it will cause 110% Oxygen Low, cause 20% Lung Damage, cause 10% Burns, and use 5% of the incendium in the tank. 
Using the ambubag with paint in it will cause 50% Oxygen Low, cause 3% lung damage, cause 5% chemical addiction, treat 20% chemical withdrawal, and use 5% of the paint in the can. 
Using the ambubag with Analgesic Tank will give Analgesia, Opiate Addiction, Opiate Overdose, and Opiate Withdrawal. The amount depends on whether the patient is drunk or not.
Infotexts.ntg.effects.ambubag: [oxygen_low] Treat Oxygen Low by 90% (Oxygen Tank / Oxygenite Tank) or cause 110% Oxygen Low (Welding Fuel Tank / Incendium Fuel Tank) | [respiratory_arrest] Cure Respiratory Arrest (Oxygenite Tank) | [lung_damage] Cause 10% Lung Damage (Welding Fuel Tank) or 20% Lung Damage (Incendium Fuel Tank) | [burns] Cause 10% Burns (Incendium Fuel Tank) | [withdrawal] Treat 20% Chemical Withdrawal (Paint Can) | [analgesia] Cause Analgesia (Analgesic Tank)
Infotexts.ntg.description.analgesic_tank: A tank of analgesics which can be used in the Surgery Table or Ambubag. It can be bought or crafted, and refilled. It can receive quality, increasing its maximum condition by 5% per level. \nWhen used in a Surgery Table, provides up to 14% Analgesia to the patient laying on the table, and its condition reduces by 0.7% per second.
Infotexts.ntg.applicationsuccess.analgesic_tank: [analgesia] +56% Analgesia (106% if Drunk) | +40% Opiate Addiction | [opiate_overdose] +30% Opiate Overdose (60% if Drunk) | -75% Opiate Withdrawal
Infotexts.ntg.description.anaparalyzant: Medical skill check: 64. \n\nAlongside it's original use of providing paralysis resistance and treating paralysis, Anaparalyzant can be used to quickly decrease Anesthesia and wake a patient up much faster.
Infotexts.ntg.applicationsuccess.anaparalyzant: [anesthesia] Instantly cures Anesthesia | Cures Paralysis | +5% Psychosis | +800% Paralysis Resistance
Infotexts.ntg.description.antibiotic_glue: Medical skill check: 55. \nA glue used to quickly seal bleeding wounds and treat Burns on a single limb. Also treats Infected Wounds.
Infotexts.ntg.applicationfailed.antibiotic_glue: [bleeding] -40% Bleeding | [burns] -10% Burns | [infected_wounds] -100% Infected Wounds | [heart_attack] 50% chance to cause Heart Attack
Infotexts.ntg.description.antibiotic_ointment: Medical skill check: 10. \nAntibiotic ointment is an item used to heal Burns and treat Infected Wounds.
Infotexts.ntg.description.antiseptic: Antiseptic is an item whose only use is to be loaded into the Antiseptic Sprayer. For each spray of the antiseptic sprayer, 10% condition will be used from the antiseptic.
Infotexts.ntg.description.antiseptic_sprayer: A sprayer which sprays Antiseptic onto Infected Wounds. Using the antiseptic sprayer with antiseptic in it will treat 100% infected wounds, give 5% Ointmented and use up 10% of the antiseptic.
Infotexts.ntg.description.arm_item: A replacement Left or Right arm that can be attached to a matching surgically amputated and prepared arm socket. \nSevered arms require refrigeration or they will expire. Bionic arms are synthetic and don't require refrigeration, but are otherwise biologically equivalent to natural arms.
Infotexts.ntg.seealso.arm_item: [gangrene] Gangrene | [traumatic_amputation] Traumatic Amputation | [surgical_amputation] Surgical Amputation | [amputation_surgery] Amputation Surgery
Infotexts.ntg.description.aed: An item used to correct the heartbeat of a patient. When used on a patient and charged with batteries, the aed has a chance to either reset Fibrillation, or cure Cardiac Arrest. \nAt medical skill below 80, the Manual Defibrillator is worse in all regards. At 100 medical skill, the manual defibrillator is better in all regards.
Infotexts.ntg.description.autopulse: A medic's best friend. To use the autopulse, simply wear it in your diving suit slot. \nWhile worn and equipped with batteries, the autopulse will cure Cardiac Arrest and slightly treat Oxygen Low and Fibrillation, as well as increasing your blood pressure. Be wary to not overuse it, however, as it will increase your blood pressure into Hypertension . It can be forcibly removed by using the Wrench as a treatment on the torso of a person wearing it, as long as the person is not Unconsciousness. Doing so will place it in the inventory of the wrench user.
Infotexts.ntg.seealso.autopulse: [cardiac_arrest] Cardiac Arrest | [oxygen_low] Oxygen Low | [mechanic_fibrillation] Fibrillation | [hypertension] Hypertension | [wrench] Wrench
Infotexts.ntg.description.azathioprine: Medical Skill Check: 10 \nAzathioprine is an immunosuppressant medication used during Organ Transplant Surgery if organ rejection is enabled in Neurotrauma's config. It will lower Immunity over time, making it safe to place an organ into the body when Immunity is below 10%.
Infotexts.ntg.applicationfailed.azathioprine: [immunity] Lowers immunity over time | [sepsis] Causes Sepsis
Infotexts.ntg.description.bandages: Medical skill check: 10. \nBandages are used for covering wounds to prevent Infected Wounds. Bandages can also stabilize Dislocations preventing internal damage as well as stabilizing Fractures to prevent Internal Damage, Pneumothorax and Neurotrauma for Rib and Skull fractures respectively.
Infotexts.ntg.description.blood_packs: Blood packs are acquired from using an Empty Blood Pack on a human with less than 31% Blood Loss. Blood packs must be administered to the correct bloodtype, which is stated in the description of the item. \nIf the human that the blood pack came from was suffering from either Acidosis, Alkalosis, or Sepsis, then those afflictions will be stored in the blood pack (1/5 of the donor's affliction strength). Placing a blood pack in a Hematology Analyzer will show if it has any of these afflictions and their strength. \nAdditionally, if a recipient has acidosis or alkalosis, administering neutral blood will multiply it by 0.9, unless they have 0% bloodloss.
Infotexts.ntg.applicationfailed.blood_packs: [blood_loss] -30% Blood Loss | +30% Blood Pressure | [hemotransfusion_shock] +100% Hemotransfusion Shock
Infotexts.ntg.description.blue_shark: A funny little blue shark that might just cure your mental instability. Treats 2% Psychosis if used in the Health UI.
Infotexts.ntg.description.body_bag: The Body Bag is an item used to transport bodies much more quickly than simply dragging them. It is placed into the Diving Suit slot and will suffocate a living human if they are placed into it. \nIt can be forcibly removed by using the Wrench as a treatment on the torso of a person wearing it, as long as the person is not Unconsciousness. Doing so will place it in the inventory of the wrench user.
Infotexts.ntg.description.brain_transplant:
Medical skill check: 100 
A vital organ. The patient's consciousness and Neurotrauma are directly linked to it. Unlike other organs, the brain is typically not transplanted. 
The brain may be removed or transplanted during an Organ Transplant Surgery done on the head. Removal of the brain causes immediate Unconsciousness, Cardiac Arrest, Respiratory Arrest, loss of character control (the patient will enter freecam), and 100% Neurotrauma, and continuous rapid neurotrauma gain, all of which will persist as long as the organ is removed. The condition of the organ is inversely proportional to neurotrauma, up to 100%. Removing a brain at 100% or more neurotrauma will not produce a brain transplant and the patient will immediately reach 200% neurotrauma, killing them.
