If the brain is transplanted back into the patient, they will regain control of the character, but their Cardiac Arrest and Respiratory Arrest must still be treated. If the brain is transplanted into another patient, the donor will take control of that patient. 
Unlike other organs, the condition of the brain will always decay rapidly, even in a refrigerated container, unless it is placed in a Brain Jar. If the brain or the container it is in ever touches the ground, it will be destroyed, unless if it is in a brain jar.
Infotexts.ntg.seealso.brain_transplant: [organ_transplant_surgery] Organ Transplant Surgery | [neurotrauma] Neurotrauma | [unconsciousness] Unconsciousness | [cardiac_arrest] Cardiac Arrest | [respiratory_arrest] Respiratory Arrest | [organ_rejection] Organ Rejection | [immunity] Immunity | [refrigerated_container] Refrigerated Container | [refrigerated_crate] Refrigerated Crate
Infotexts.ntg.description.abx:
Medical skill check: 25 
Broad-spectrum Antibiotics are the only treatment for Sepsis. Still applies the husk infection resistance buff and reduces existing husk infection over time as per vanilla. 
A successful use of the antibiotics will provide an affliction strength of 50%, which will decrease at 0.5% a second, totaling for 100 seconds with one dose. 50% Broad-spectrum Antibiotics will treat 0.95% Sepsis per second for 100 seconds. A failed use will provide an affliction strength of 30%. It will decrease and treat Sepsis at the same rate as a successful use. 30% Broad-spectrum Antibiotics will treat 0.95% Sepsis per second for 60 seconds. 
In total, the organs will end up with 20% Organ Damage, 17.5% Kidney and Liver Damage, and 10% Heart and Lung Damage on successful application and 12% Organ Damage, 10.5% Kidney and Liver Damage, and 6% Heart and Lung Damage on failed application
Infotexts.ntg.applicationsuccess.abx: [vanilla_organ_damage] +0.2%/s Vanilla Organ Damage for 100s | [kidney_damage] +0.175%/s Kidney Damage for 100s | [liver_damage] +0.175%/s Liver Damage for 100s | [heart_damage] +0.1%/s Heart Damage for 100s | [lung_damage] +0.1%/s Lung Damage for 100s | [sepsis] -0.95%/s Sepsis for 100s
Infotexts.ntg.applicationfailed.abx: [vanilla_organ_damage] +0.2%/s Vanilla Organ Damage for 60s | [kidney_damage] +0.175%/s Kidney Damage for 60s | [liver_damage] +0.175%/s Liver Damage for 60s | [heart_damage] +0.1%/s Heart Damage for 60s | [lung_damage] +0.1%/s Lung Damage for 60s | [sepsis] -0.95%/s Sepsis for 60s
Infotexts.ntg.description.combat_stimulant: Medical Skill: 35 \nCombat Stimulant is a stimulant that also doubles as a first aid item. It has the same effects as vanilla, with additional afflictions treated. A successful application will give 20% Chem Addiction and 75% Combat Stimulant; a failed one will give 40% Chem Addiction instead. It will also grant between 45% to 50% Stun Resistance. If between 1% to 10% Combat Stimulant, the treatment strength is halved and the Stun Resistance will be between 45% to 0%. The following effects will be given by the Combat Stimulant affliction if between 11% to 100% Strength:
Infotexts.ntg.applicationsuccess.combat_stimulant: [burns] -2%/s Burns | [blood_loss] -2%/s Blood Loss | [bleeding] -2%/s Bleeding | [internal_bleeding] -2%/s Internal Bleeding | [internal_wounds] -2%/s Internal Wounds | [open_wounds] -2%/s Open Wounds | [sutures] -2%/s Stitched Wounds | [concussion] -2%/s Concussion | [withdrawal] -2%/s Chem Withdrawal |
Infotexts.ntg.description.deusizine: Medical skill check: 72 \nA special drug, treating a variety of life-threatening afflictions over 20 seconds and providing a moderate Vigor buff.
Infotexts.ntg.applicationsuccess.deusizine: [internal_wounds] -20% Internal Damage | [blood_loss] -50% Blood Loss | [bleeding] -50% Bleeding | [hypoxemia] -100% Hypoxemia | [vanilla_organ_damage] -20% Vanilla Organ Damage | +40% Blood Pressure | +200% Vigor
Infotexts.ntg.applicationfailed.deusizine: [internal_wounds] -10% Internal Damage | [blood_loss] -50% Blood Loss | [bleeding] -50% Bleeding | [hypoxemia] -40% Hypoxemia | [vanilla_organ_damage] -10% Vanilla Organ Damage | +40% Blood Pressure | +200% Vigor
Infotexts.ntg.description.dialysis_filter: A filter which can be placed in the Surgery Table to reduce the patient's Acidosis or Alkalosis. It can be crafted, but cannot be bought. It can receive quality, increasing its maximum condition by 5% per level. \nIf the patient lays on the table with an installed dialysis filter and has either Acidosis or Alkalosis, these afflictions will be reduced by 0.8/s, the condition of the filter will drop by 1/s, and there is a 0.1% chance per second for the patient to receive Sepsis. The condition of the filter will not drop and there is no chance of sepsis if the patient does not have acidosis or alkalosis.
Infotexts.ntg.description.diving_knife: Medical Skill Check: 30 \nIn addition to the normal uses of a knife, they can be used to remove Bandages.
Infotexts.ntg.description.drainage: A Drainage is a one-time use item that treats Pneumothorax during Open / Close Surgery. If the patient does not have pneumothorax, the drainage will do nothing and will not be consumed.
Infotexts.ntg.description.empty_blood_pack:
Medical skill check: 30

Empty blood packs are used for acquiring Blood Packs. Empty blood packs can only be used on humans with less than 31% Blood Loss, otherwise it does nothing. 
If the human that the empty blood pack is used on is suffering from either Acidosis, Alkalosis or Sepsis, then those afflictions will also be given to whoever the blood is given to (affliction gained will be the donor's affliction level at time of extraction divided by 5). Using an empty blood pack will multiply the patient's acidosis or alkalosis by 0.9.
Infotexts.ntg.description.endovascular_balloon: The third step in Aortic Rupture Surgery. The balloon will not work without 100% Surgery Incision. Using the balloon will block the aorta, which will prevent Blood Loss caused by Aortic Rupture, and will treat regular bleeding, but will also cause Gangrene.
Infotexts.ntg.seealso.endovascular_balloon: [aortic_rupture_surgery] Aortic Rupture Surgery | [surgery_incision] Surgery Incision | [blood_loss] Blood Loss | [aortic_rupture] Aortic Rupture | [gangrene] Gangrene
Infotexts.ntg.description.fentanyl: Medical skill check: 72 \nA powerful opiate used to induce Analgesia. \nTo prevent exploits, fentanyl cannot be shot as a projectile, such as out of a syringe gun.
Infotexts.ntg.applicationsuccess.fentanyl: [analgesia] +75% Analgesia (150% if Drunk) | +15% Opiate Addiction | [opiate_overdose] +22.5% Opiate Overdose (37.5% if Drunk) | [withdrawal] -100% Opiate Withdrawal
Infotexts.ntg.applicationfailed.fentanyl: [analgesia] +50% Analgesia (100% if Drunk) | +40% Opiate Addiction | [opiate_overdose] +30% Opiate Overdose (60% if Drunk) | [withdrawal] -100% Opiate Withdrawal
Infotexts.ntg.description.gel_ice_pack: Medical Skill Check: 40 \nA reusable cold pack used to promote the healing of blunt force trauma and for the treatment of internal bleeding. Usage will decrease its condition along with leaving it outside of refrigeration, but its condition will be restored if refrigerated. \nEven if its condition reaches 0% from usage or being left outside, its condition will increase once refrigerated.
Infotexts.ntg.description.gypsum: Medical skill check: 40 \nA fine powder that when used in conjunction with Bandaged, will encase a limb in a Plaster Cast, in order to heal Fractures. Gypsum will not work if you have not used Bandages or Plastiseal beforehand, and will do nothing for skull, rib and neck fractures. \nFor each plaster cast applied to the player, they will get a 20% movement speed debuff. Plaster casts can be removed with Trauma Shears.
Infotexts.ntg.applicationsuccess.gypsum: [plaster_cast] Turns the Bandage into a Plaster Cast
Infotexts.ntg.applicationfailed.gypsum: Deletes the Gypsum and does nothing
Infotexts.ntg.description.haloperidol: Medical skill check: 37 \nAn antipsychotic drug also useful in the treatment of alcohol withdrawal.
Infotexts.ntg.description.health_scanner: An item which is extremely important for diagnosis. Upon use and charged with batteries, you will get the readout of all afflictions visible to the health scanner, and the patient you're scanning will gain 0.5% Radiation Sickness per second, and you (the person scanning) will gain 0.3% per second. \nAdditionally, one can scan themselves and others by using the health scanner in the interface like a treatment, as you would a bandage. This gives 1% radiation sickness, and also allows you to scan individual limbs to find things such as Foreign Bodies.
Infotexts.ntg.description.hematology_analyzer:
The hematology analyzer can also be used to check the blood type of your patient, to avoid Hemotransfusion Shock. Putting a donor card in the analyzer and then using it will print their blood type onto the donor card, and put it in their id card slot, allowing you to check their blood type later without the analyzer. 
Putting a blood pack in the analyzer will give a readout of its type and any of the aforementioned ailments, if it has any.

Either failing or passing the skill check will also give you a readout of all the blood-related ailments your patient has, such as Sepsis, Acidosis or Alkalosis.
Infotexts.ntg.description.hemostat: The third step in most surgical procedures. The hemostat will not work without 100% Surgery Incision. Using it requires no medical skill. Successfully using the hemostat will give the patient Clamped Bleeding on the limb the tool was used on. \nThis item is not directly craftable. To obtain it from a fabricator, the player must craft the “Surgery toolbox (kit)”.
Infotexts.ntg.description.hyperzine: Medical Skill Check: 50. \nA Powerful stimulant. Haste and Vigor are applied instantly, while the other effects are applied over a 60 second duration.
Infotexts.ntg.applicationsuccess.hyperzine: [internal_damage] -12% Internal Damage | +18% Chemical Addiction | [neurotrauma] +18% Neurotrauma | +15% Psychosis | +400% Haste | +400% Vigor | -54% Stun | [withdrawal] -90% Chemical Withdrawal
Infotexts.ntg.applicationfailed.hyperzine: [internal_damage] -16% Internal Damage | +36% Chemical Addiction | [neurotrauma] +36% Neurotrauma | +30% Psychosis | +400% Haste | +400% Vigor | -27% Stun | [withdrawal] -90% Chemical Withdrawal
Infotexts.ntg.description.heart_transplant:
Medical skill check: 40 
A vital organ. The patient's heart-related afflictions, such as Heart Damage, are directly linked to it. 
The heart may be removed or transplanted during an Organ Transplant Surgery done on the torso. Removal of the heart causes immediate Cardiac Arrest, 100% Heart Damage, and up to 20% Vanilla Organ Damage, inversely proportional to heart damage before removal. The condition of the organ is inversely proportional to heart damage before removal. The patient cannot gain and will be immediately relieved of Cardiac Tamponade and Heart Attack while their heart is removed. 
If a heart is transplanted back into the patient, their Heart Damage and up to 20% Vanilla Organ Damage, both inversely proportional to the condition of the organ, will be treated, but their Cardiac Arrest will not be treated. The patient may gain Organ Rejection if they receive the organ and their Immunity is higher than 10%, if the mechanic is toggled on in the mod config. 
The condition of a heart will decay unless placed in a Refrigerated Container or Refrigerated Crate. While refrigerated, the organ will slowly regain condition if it is above 90%, otherwise it will not change. If the organ or the container it is in hits the ground from a large height, it will be destroyed.
Infotexts.ntg.seealso.heart_transplant: [organ_transplant_surgery] Organ Transplant Surgery | [heart_damage] Heart Damage | [cardiac_arrest] Cardiac Arrest | [vanilla_organ_damage] Vanilla Organ Damage | [cardiac_tamponade] Cardiac Tamponade | [heart_attack] Heart Attack | [organ_rejection] Organ Rejection | [immunity] Immunity | [refrigerated_container] Refrigerated Container | [refrigerated_crate] Refrigerated Crate
Infotexts.ntg.description.kidney_transplant:
Medical skill check: 40. 
A pair of vital organs. The patient's Kidney Damage is directly related to it. Unlike other organs, patients will have two kidneys, which are individually damaged. At 50% kidney damage, only one kidney has failed, while the other kidney will be functional. A patient may be healthy with only one kidney. By itself, 50% kidney damage will not cause lethal Hypertension. 
The kidneys may be removed or transplanted during an Organ Transplant Surgery done on the torso. Removing the kidneys will remove both at once, causing 100% Kidney Damage and up to 20% Vanilla Organ Damage, inversely proportional to kidney damage before removal. The condition of the organs are inversely proportional to kidney damage before removal, up to 50% for each kidney. Therefore, removal of the kidneys at 25% kidney damage will produce one kidney at 100% and another one at 50%. 
If the kidneys are transplanted back into the patient, up to 50% Kidney Damage and up to 10% Vanilla Organ Damage per kidney, both inversely proportional to their condition, will be treated. The patient may gain Organ Rejection if they receive the organ and their Immunity is higher than 10%, if the mechanic is enabled in the mod config. 
The condition of a kidney will decay unless placed in a Refrigerated Container or Refrigerated Crate. While refrigerated, the organ will slowly regain condition if it is above 90%, otherwise it will not change. If the organ or the container it is in is dropped from a large height, it will be destroyed.
Infotexts.ntg.seealso.kidney_transplant: [organ_transplant_surgery] Organ Transplant Surgery | [kidney_damage] Kidney Damage | [hypertension] Hypertension | [vanilla_organ_damage] Vanilla Organ Damage | [organ_rejection] Organ Rejection | [immunity] Immunity | [refrigerated_container] Refrigerated Container | [refrigerated_crate] Refrigerated Crate
Infotexts.ntg.description.leg_item: A replacement Left or Right leg that can be attached to a matching surgically amputated and prepared leg socket. \nSevered legs require refrigeration or they will expire. Bionic legs are synthetic and don't require refrigeration, but are otherwise biologically equivalent to natural legs.
Infotexts.ntg.seealso.leg_item: [gangrene] Gangrene | [traumatic_amputation] Traumatic Amputation | [surgical_amputation] Surgical Amputation | [amputation_surgery] Amputation Surgery
Infotexts.ntg.description.liver_transplant:
Medical skill check: 40

A vital organ. The patient's Liver Damage is directly linked to it. 
The liver may be removed or transplanted during an Organ Transplant Surgery done on the torso. Removal of the liver causes 100% Liver Damage and up to 20% Vanilla Organ Damage, inversely proportional to liver damage before removal. The condition of the organ is inversely proportional to liver damage before removal. 
If a liver is transplanted back into the patient, their Liver Damage and up to 20% Vanilla Organ Damage, both inversely proportional to the condition of the organ, will be treated. The patient may gain Organ Rejection if they receive the organ and their Immunity is higher than 10%, if the mechanic is toggled on in the mod config.

The condition of a liver will decay unless placed in a Refrigerated Container or Refrigerated Crate. While refrigerated, the organ will slowly regain condition if it is above 90%, otherwise it will not change. If the organ or the container it is in hits the ground from a large height, it will be destroyed.
Infotexts.ntg.seealso.liver_transplant: [organ_transplant_surgery] Organ Transplant Surgery | [liver_damage] Liver Damage | [vanilla_organ_damage] Vanilla Organ Damage | [organ_rejection] Organ Rejection | [immunity] Immunity | [refrigerated_container] Refrigerated Container | [refrigerated_crate] Refrigerated Crate
Infotexts.ntg.description.liquid_oxygenite: Medical skill check: 72. \nA liquified alien material used to inject oxygen directly into the bloodstream, treating Hypoxemia. On application success, user is functionally immune to Hypoxemia. \nAll effects listed below apply over the course of 30 seconds.
Infotexts.ntg.applicationsuccess.liquid_oxygenite: [hypoxemia] -3000% Hypoxemia | [vanilla_organ_damage] +6% Vanilla Organ Damage | [lung_damage] +6% Lung Damage | [liver_damage] +6% Liver Damage | [heart_damage] +6% Heart Damage | [kidney_damage] +6% Kidney Damage
Infotexts.ntg.applicationfailed.liquid_oxygenite: [hypoxemia] -200% Hypoxemia | [vanilla_organ_damage] +10% Vanilla Organ Damage | [lung_damage] +10% Lung Damage | [liver_damage] +10% Liver Damage | [heart_damage] +10% Heart Damage | [kidney_damage] +10% Kidney Damage
Infotexts.ntg.description.lung_transplant:
Medical skill check: 40. 
A vital organ. The patient's lung-related afflictions, such as Lung Damage, are directly linked to it. 
The lungs may be removed or transplanted during an Organ Transplant Surgery done on the torso. Removal of the lungs causes immediate Respiratory Arrest, 100% Lung Damage, and up to 20% Vanilla Organ Damage, inversely proportional to lung damage before removal. The condition of the organ is inversely proportional to lung damage before removal. The patient cannot gain and will be immediately relieved of Pneumothorax, and will gain Oxygen Low even if they are receiving artificial ventilation or wearing an AutoPulse, while their lungs are removed. 
If the lungs are transplanted back into the patient, their Lung Damage and up to 20% Vanilla Organ Damage, both inversely proportional to the condition of the organ, will be treated, but their Respiratory Arrest will not be treated. The patient may gain Organ Rejection if they receive the organ and their Immunity is higher than 10%, if the mechanic is toggled on in the mod config. 
The condition of the lungs will decay unless placed in a Refrigerated Container or Refrigerated Crate. While refrigerated, the organ will slowly regain condition if it is above 90%, otherwise it will not change. If the organ or the container it is in hits the ground from a large height, it will be destroyed.
Infotexts.ntg.seealso.lung_transplant: [organ_transplant_surgery] Organ Transplant Surgery | [lung_damage] Lung Damage | [respiratory_arrest] Respiratory Arrest | [vanilla_organ_damage] Vanilla Organ Damage | [pneumothorax] Pneumothorax | [oxygen_low] Oxygen Low | [autopulse] AutoPulse | [organ_rejection] Organ Rejection | [immunity] Immunity | [refrigerated_container] Refrigerated Container | [refrigerated_crate] Refrigerated Crate
Infotexts.ntg.description.mannitol: Medical skill check: 60. \nMannitol is a medication used to treat Neurotrauma and halve its gain. In return, it causes various organ damage. For mannitol to treat Neurotrauma, blood pressure must be greater than 70% and Hypoxemia must be less than 30%. \nEffects occur over the course of 10 seconds.
Infotexts.ntg.applicationsuccess.mannitol: [neurotrauma] +50% Mannitol (Treats up to 100% Neurotrauma) | [vanilla_organ_damage] +5% Vanilla Organ Damage | [heart_damage] +10% Heart Damage | [kidney_damage] +10% Kidney Damage
Infotexts.ntg.applicationfailed.mannitol: [neurotrauma] +30% Mannitol (Treats up to 60% Neurotrauma) | [vanilla_organ_damage] +10% Vanilla Organ Damage | [heart_damage] +20% Heart Damage| [kidney_damage] +20% Kidney Damage
Infotexts.ntg.description.manual_defibrillator: An item used to correct the heartbeat of a patient. When used on a patient and charged with batteries, the defibrillator has a chance to either reset Fibrillation, cause Cardiac Arrest, or cure cardiac arrest. Using any defibrillator on a human will stun them for one second. \nAt medical skill below 80, the Automated External Defibrillator (AED) has better chances in all regards. At 100 medical skill, the manual defibrillator is superior. Manual defibrillator becomes perfectly safe, and is guaranteed to cure cardiac arrest and fibrillation.
Infotexts.ntg.seealso.manual_defibrillator: [aed] Automated External Defibrillator (AED) | [mechanic_fibrillation] Fibrillation | [cardiac_arrest] Cardiac Arrest
Infotexts.ntg.description.medical_stent: The fourth step in Aortic Rupture Surgery. The stent will not work if you have not used the Endovascular Balloon beforehand. Using the medical stent will cure Aortic Rupture, and also remove the endovascular balloon from the patient.
Infotexts.ntg.seealso.medical_stent: [endovascular_balloon] Endovascular Balloon | [aortic_rupture_surgery] Aortic Rupture Surgery | [aortic_rupture] Aortic Rupture
Infotexts.ntg.description.methamphetamine: Medical Skill Check: 35. \nA stimulant. Hyperactivity occurs instantly, while the other effects occur over 30 seconds.
Infotexts.ntg.applicationsuccess.methamphetamine: +70% Hyperactivity | [vanilla_organ_damage] +15% Vanilla Organ Damage | [neurotrauma] +15% Neurotrauma | +15% Chemical Addiction | +30% Psychosis | -22.5% Stun | [withdrawal] -90% Chemical Withdrawal
Infotexts.ntg.applicationfailed.methamphetamine: +50% Hyperactivity | [vanilla_organ_damage] +30% Vanilla Organ Damage | [neurotrauma] +30% Neurotrauma | +30% Chemical Addiction | +45% Psychosis | -22.5% Stun | [withdrawal] -90% Chemical Withdrawal
Infotexts.ntg.description.morphine: Medical skill check: 30. \nA moderately powerful opiate used to induce Analgesia. To prevent exploits, morphine cannot be shot as a projectile, such as out of a syringe gun.
Infotexts.ntg.applicationsuccess.morphine: [analgesia] +50% Analgesia (100% if Drunk) | [opiate_overdose] +10% Opiate Overdose (20% if Drunk) | +10% Opiate Addiction | [withdrawal] -30% Opiate Withdrawal
Infotexts.ntg.applicationfailed.morphine: [analgesia] +30% Analgesia (60% if Drunk) | [opiate_overdose] +40% Opiate Overdose | +10% Opiate Addiction | [withdrawal] -30% Opiate Withdrawal
Infotexts.ntg.description.naloxone: Medical skill check: 39. \nNaloxone is used to treat the effects of opiates.
Infotexts.ntg.applicationsuccess.naloxone: [withdrawal] -60% Opiate Withdrawal | [opiate_overdose] -60% Opiate Overdose | [analgesia] -60% Analgesia | -60% Opiate Addiction
Infotexts.ntg.applicationfailed.naloxone: [withdrawal] -30% Opiate Withdrawal | [opiate_overdose] -30% Opiate Overdose | [analgesia] -30% Analgesia | -60% Opiate Addiction | [coma] +15% Coma (50% Chance)
Infotexts.ntg.description.needle: Medical skill check: 20. \nA consumable needle that reduces the strength of Pneumothorax to 5%, but does not treat it. If the patient does not have pneumothorax, the needle will give them pneumothorax instead.
Infotexts.ntg.applicationsuccess.needle: [pneumothorax] Reduces to Pneumothorax 5% and prevents worsening
Infotexts.ntg.description.nitroglycerin: Medical skill check: 35. \nA jar containing a very volatile vasodilator, liable to explode on impact or exposure to heat. While this drug is active, Fibrillation caused by Hypotension will be reduced. Identical effect and affliction to Sodium Nitroprusside.
Infotexts.ntg.description.opium: Medical skill check: 30. \nA weak opiate used to induce Analgesia.
Infotexts.ntg.applicationsuccess.opium: [analgesia] +20% Analgesia (40% if Drunk) | [opiate_overdose] +5% Opiate Overdose (18.75% if Drunk) | +10% Opiate Addiction | [withdrawal] -15% Opiate Withdrawal
Infotexts.ntg.applicationfailed.opium: [analgesia] +10% Analgesia (20% if Drunk) | [opiate_overdose] +15% Opiate Overdose (30% if Drunk) | +10% Opiate Addiction | [withdrawal] -15% Opiate Withdrawal
Infotexts.ntg.description.multipurpose_scalpel: The Multipurpose Scalpel combines the functionality of the regular scalpel, the organ procurement scalpels, and the trauma shears, while also providing the user with a way of intentionally harming the patient. \nThis item is craftable at a regular fabricator. \nThe mode of this Scalpel is changed by equipping the item (akin to a weapon or tool) and clicking on the available UI buttons which appear.
Infotexts.ntg.description.multipurpose_scalpel_ex1: In this mode, the item behaves identically to a regular Scalpel.
Infotexts.ntg.description.multipurpose_scalpel_ex2: In this mode, as long as the patient has a bandage or a cast, the item functions identically to Trauma Shears. \nIs this not the case, then using it will result in the patient getting stabbed. Depending on location and surgical status of the patient, this can cause serious damage.
Infotexts.ntg.description.multipurpose_scalpel_ex3: In this mode, the item will have the ability to extract any organ from a patient, and it is a required item for Organ Transplant Surgery. The organ extracted depends on which limb has Retracted Skin, and which limb the Multipurpose Scalpel is used on:| [kidney_transplant] Apply to Left Arm, with retracted skin on the Torso | [liver_transplant] Apply to Torso, with retracted skin on the Torso | [heart_transplant] Apply to Right Arm, with retracted skin on the Torso | [lung_transplant] Apply to Left Leg, with retracted skin on the Torso | [brain_transplant] Apply to Head, with retracted skin on the Head
Infotexts.ntg.description.osteosynthesis_implants: Medical skill check: 45. \nThe sixth step in Osteosynthetic Surgery. The implants will not work without 100% Drilled Bones.
Infotexts.ntg.applicationsuccess.osteosynthesis_im: [fractures] Cures all fractures on limb using up 25% of the implant's condition
Infotexts.ntg.description.plastiseal: Medical skill check: 22. \nAn improved version of Bandaged, which can more effectively treat wounds. \nApplying plastiseal will also replace any dirty bandages on the limb the bandage is applied to. Plastiseal can also be used after Retracted Skin in order to treat third degree burns.
Infotexts.ntg.description.propofol:
Propofol is no longer craftable. 
A drug used to induce controlled Unconsciousness for the purpose of preventing Traumatic Shock. 
Upon use, the patient will gain Anesthesia at 0.3% per second. Between 1-15% anesthesia, the patient will have 5% Analgesia. Beyond 15% anesthesia, the patient will be unconscious. At 100% anesthesia, the patient will no longer be under the effect of propofol. 
Application of Anaparalyzant will reduce anesthesia to 0%, also removing its effect.
Infotexts.ntg.sideeffects.propofol: [hypotension] Hypotension | [confusion] Confusion | [blurred_vision] Blurred Vision | [fever] Fever | [mechanic_fibrillation] Fibrillation | [seizure] Seizure | [vomiting_blood] Vomiting Blood | Psychosis
Infotexts.ntg.seealso.propofol: [anaparalyzant] Anaparalyzant | [analgesia] Analgesia | [anesthesia] Anesthesia | [unconsciousness] Unconsciousness | [traumatic_shock] Traumatic Shock
Infotexts.ntg.description.raptor_bane_extract: Raptor Bane Extract is used in the treatment of Acidosis by inducing Vomiting in the patient to give them Alkalosis.
Infotexts.ntg.description.refrigerated_container: A container which shares many properties with the Storage Container. It has 6 slots and may hold any small item. However, its main use is to hold organ transplants (except for the Brain Transplant), severed limbs, and Gel Ice Packs. This will prevent the items from naturally losing condition. Organ transplants will slowly regain condition if they are above 90%, limbs above 95%, and gel ice packs at any condition. \nOrgan transplants may be destroyed if they are placed in the container and it is dropped from a large height.
Infotexts.ntg.seealso.refrigerated_container: [heart_transplant] Heart Transplant | [kidney_transplant] Kidney Transplant | [liver_transplant] Liver Transplant | [lung_transplant] Lung Transplant | [arm_item] Arm (Severed) | [leg_item] Leg (Severed) | [gel_ice_pack] Gel Ice Pack
Infotexts.ntg.description.refrigerated_crate: A container which shares many properties with waterproof Crates. It has 12 slots and may hold any small item. However, its main use is to hold organ transplants (except for the Brain Transplant), severed limbs, and Gel Ice Packs. This will prevent the items from naturally losing condition. Organ transplants will slowly regain condition if they are above 90%, limbs above 95%, and gel ice packs at any condition. \nOrgan transplants may be destroyed if they are placed in the crate and it is dropped from a large height.
Infotexts.ntg.seealso.refrigerated_crate: [heart_transplant] Heart Transplant | [kidney_transplant] Kidney Transplant | [liver_transplant] Liver Transplant | [lung_transplant] Lung Transplant | [arm_item] Arm (Severed) | [leg_item] Leg (Severed) | [gel_ice_pack] Gel Ice Pack
Infotexts.ntg.description.ringers_solution: Medical Skill Check: 20\n\nAn isotonic solution used to replace lost fluids. Increases blood pressure without other effects.
Infotexts.ntg.description.saline: Medical Skill Check: 10. \nAn isotonic solution used to replace lost fluids or treat Alkalosis.
Infotexts.ntg.description.scalpel: Medical skill check: 30. \nThe second step in all surgical procedures. The scalpel will not work without Analgesia or Unconsciousness. \nThis item is not directly craftable. To obtain it from a fabricator, the player must craft the “Surgery toolbox (kit)”.
Infotexts.ntg.applicationsuccess.scalpel: [surgery_incision] Applies Surgery Incision and removes Bandages/Casts
Infotexts.ntg.description.skin_retractors: Medical skill check: 30. \nThe fourth step in most surgical procedures. The retractors will not work without 100% Clamped Bleeding. \nThis item is not directly craftable. To obtain it from a fabricator, the player must craft the “Surgery toolbox (kit)”.
Infotexts.ntg.description.sodium_nitroprusside: Medical skill check: 10. \nA medication to reduce blood pressure. While this drug is active, Fibrillation caused by Hypotension will be reduced. Identical effect and affliction to Nitroglycerin.
Infotexts.ntg.applicationsuccess.spinal_cord_impla: [spinal_cord_injury] -100% Spinal Cord Injury (treat the fracture too!)
Infotexts.ntg.description.stasis_bag:
A special bag that keeps the body in stasis for transportation to proper treatment if injuries are severe. A stasis bag is worn in the diving suit slot, and wearing it will drain the condition of it by 0.25% per second, meaning a stasis bag will last for 400 seconds or roughly 6 and a half minutes. 
Wearing a stasis bag will protect the wearer from pressure down to 7,000 meters and will allow the wearer to be dragged at full speed. Additionally, the wearer's Husk Infection will be reduced by 0.15/s, if it is lower than 100%. 
Patients in a Stasis Bag cannot receive surgery, and will not be able to receive surgery for 3 seconds after being removed from the bag. They can still receive items such as drugs, however. 
Patients removed from the Stasis Bag will have Cardiac Arrest, Respiratory Arrest and Unconsciousness and will need those afflictions treated. 
The following afflictions are halted by the stasis bag:
Infotexts.ntg.effects.stasis_bag: [cardiac_arrest] Cardiac Arrest | [respiratory_arrest] Respiratory Arrest | [heart_attack] Heart Attack | [neurotrauma] Neurotrauma | [kidney_damage] Kidney Damage | [liver_damage] Liver Damage | [heart_damage] Heart Damage | [lung_damage] Lung Damage | [bone_damage] Bone Damage | [sepsis] Sepsis | [hypoxemia] Hypoxemia | [hypotension] Hypotension | [hypertension] Hypertension | [internal_bleeding] Internal Bleeding | [stroke] Stroke | [seizure] Seizure | [coma] Coma | [infected_wounds] Infected Wounds | [bleeding] Bleeding
Infotexts.ntg.description.streptokinase: Medical skill check: None. \nA blood thinner. Using streptokinase will prevent natural bleeding regeneration and increases the chance of getting a Stroke if the patient has more than 150% blood pressure.
Infotexts.ntg.effects.streptokinase: [heart_attack] Fully treats Heart Attack | [hemotransfusion_shock] Fully treats Hemotransfusion Shock
Infotexts.ntg.description.surgery_table:
The Surgery Table is an item that can be bought from a station or crafted and then placed down. The patient is not required to be on the table, they only need to be near it. 
The Surgery Table provides artificial ventilation to everyone near the table and prevents Traumatic Shock provided the patient is under Analgesia. Additionally, the table will decrease Oxygen Low and prevent Respiratory Arrest and Hypoventilation from increasing Acidosis. The Hospital Bed has these same functions. Other bunks prevent Traumatic Shock, but only for the character laying on them, and do not provide the other effects of a Surgery Table. 
The table also has wiring pins that can be used to output important information, such as blood pressure, Acidosis, heart rate, and breathing rate. 
The table has slots for three items: an Analgesic Tank, either a Dialysis Filter or Toxin Filter and a Refrigerated Crate. 
The tank and filters only work for the patient laying on the table, and the items can only be accessed by the same patient.
Infotexts.ntg.seealso.surgery_table: [traumatic_shock] Traumatic Shock | [analgesia] Analgesia | [oxygen_low] Oxygen Low | [respiratory_arrest] Respiratory Arrest | [hypoventilation] Hypoventilation | [analgesic_tank] Analgesic Tank | [dialysis_filter] Dialysis Filter | [toxin_filter] Toxin Filter | [refrigerated_crate] Refrigerated Crate
Infotexts.ntg.description.surgical_drill: Medical skill check: 45. \nThe fifth step in Osteosynthetic Surgery. The surgical drill will not work without 100% Retracted Skin. \nThis item is not directly craftable. To obtain it from a fabricator, the player must craft the “Surgery toolbox (kit)”.
Infotexts.ntg.description.surgical_saw: Medical skill check: 45. \nThe fifth step in Amputation Surgery. The surgical saw will not work without 100% Retracted Skin. \nThis item is not directly craftable. To obtain it from a fabricator, the player must craft the “Surgery toolbox (kit)”.
Infotexts.ntg.description.sutures: Medical skill check: 30. \nThe final step in all surgical procedures. Also very useful for curing most Wounds and Bleeding.
Infotexts.ntg.applicationsuccess.sutures: [open_wounds] -20% Open Wounds | [internal_wounds] -20% Internal Damage | [bleeding] -40% Bleeding | Removes all surgery related afflictions
Infotexts.ntg.description.thiamine: Medical Skill Check: 10. \nA container of vitamin B1 pills used to assist in organ healing. Not capable of reviving dead organs (i.e. 100% organ damage). Successful application will give +50% Thiamine taking 200 seconds to apply its effects; a failed application will give 30% Thiamine taking 120 seconds.
Infotexts.ntg.applicationsuccess.thiamine: [lung_damage] -8% Lung Damage | [heart_damage] -8% Heart Damage | [kidney_damage] -8% Kidney Damage | [liver_damage] -8% Liver Damage | [vanilla_organ_damage] -8% Vanilla Organ Damage
Infotexts.ntg.applicationfailed.thiamine: [lung_damage] -4.8% Lung Damage | [heart_damage] -4.8% Heart Damage | [kidney_damage] -4.8% Kidney Damage | [liver_damage] -4.8% Liver Damage | [vanilla_organ_damage] -4.8% Vanilla Organ Damage
Infotexts.ntg.description.tourniquet: Medical skill check: 30. \nAn item used to restrict the blood flow to prevent Blood Loss from Arterial Bleeding. It will not work without Arterial Bleeding. Tourniquets automatically get removed after Open / Close Surgery. Using a tourniquet on the head will give the victim 15% Neurotrauma , 200% oxygen low, and will not clamp carotid arterial bleeding.
Infotexts.ntg.applicationsuccess.tourniquet: [blood_loss] Prevents Arterial Blood Loss | [bleeding] Treats regular bleeding
Infotexts.ntg.applicationfailed.tourniquet: [internal_wounds] +6% Blunt Force Trauma This also happens if applied to a wound that has no Arterial Bleeding
Infotexts.ntg.seealso.tourniquet: [gangrene] Gangrene | [open_close_surgery] Open / Close Surgery [arterial_bleeding] Arterial Bleeding
Infotexts.ntg.description.toxin_filter: A filter which can be placed in the Surgery Table to treat the patient's poisons. \nOnly Poison (Affliction Type) added by vanilla are valid for the toxin filter. For the patient laying on the table, the filter reduces each poison at a unique rate depending on its strength. Multiple poisons can be filtered at once. For each poison being filtered, the condition of the filter reduces by 0.8/s, and there is a 0.1% chance per second for the patient to receive Sepsis. \nThese effects do not occur if the patient is not poisoned.
Infotexts.ntg.treatments.toxin_filter.morbusine: [morbusine_poisoning] -1.8%/s between 0% to 50% Affliction Strength | [morbusine_poisoning] -2.3%/s between 50% to 100% Affliction Strength
Infotexts.ntg.treatments.toxin_filter.cyanide: [cyanide_poisoning] -2.8% between 0% to 30% Affliction Strength | [cyanide_poisoning] -5.5%/s between 30% to 100% Affliction Strength
Infotexts.ntg.treatments.toxin_filter.sufforin: [sufforin_poisoning] -1%/s between 0% to 50% Affliction Strength | [sufforin_poisoning] -1.8%/s between 50% to 100% Affliction Strength
Infotexts.ntg.treatments.toxin_filter.deliriumine: -1.6%/s between 0% to 50% Affliction Strength | -2.3%/s between 50% to 100% Affliction Strength
Infotexts.ntg.description.trauma_shears: Medical skill check: 10. \nA pair of scissors used to remove Plaster Cast and bandages. \nThis item is not directly craftable. To obtain it from a fabricator, the player must craft the “Surgery toolbox (kit)”.
Infotexts.ntg.description.tweezers:
Medical skill check: 30. 
A pair of tweezers used to remove damaged tissue or bullets. 
This item is not directly craftable. To obtain it from a fabricator, the player must craft the “Surgery toolbox (kit)”. 
Its effects depend on i there is Retracted Skin, a gunshot wound or deep tissue injury on the limb (surgery use case) or none of those apply (no surgery use case). 
The tweezers can work without Retracted Skin if the limb you are tweezing has gunshot wounds or deep tissue injuries, using the tweezer through gunshot wounds or deep tissue injuries will cause 5% Traumatic Shock . Do note that tweezing through gunshot wounds or deep tissue injuries only works on foreign bodies, and not on blunt force trauma. Tweezing out foreign bodies also has a 5% chance to yield scrap. 
Using tweezers in cases where it otherwise wouldn't do anything will cause you to pinch the patient instead, causing minor damage which is worse if on the head.
Infotexts.ntg.effects.tweezers: [bleeding] Minor Bleeding | [open_wounds] Minor Lacerations | [traumatic_shock] +5% Traumatic Shock (if via Gunshot Wound / Deep Tissue Injuries) | [foreign_bodies] -3% to -10% Foreign Bodies
Infotexts.ntg.applicationsuccess.tweezers: [internal_wounds] -3% to -10% Internal Damage (Surgery) | [internal_wounds] -3% to -10% Blunt Force Trauma (Surgery) | [foreign_bodies] -3% to -10% Foreign Bodies (Surgery) | [open_wounds] +5% Lacerations
Infotexts.ntg.description.wrench: Medical skill check: 60 (30 if the patient has Analgesia). \nThe wrench is used to treat Dislocations. To use, drag it onto the dislocated limb in the health interface and use it as a treatment, like you would a bandage. If the limb has no dislocation, the wrench will not do anything. \nThe wrench can also be used to remove an AutoPulse or Body Bag from a person, as long as they aren't Unconsciousness, by using the wrench on their torso as treatment. The item will be placed in the inventory of the wrench user.
Infotexts.ntg.description.open_close_surgery: The most common type of surgery. Extra steps can be done in-between, though must be done before suturing.
Infotexts.ntg.steps.open_close_surgery: [analgesia] 1a. Analgesia | [anesthesia] 1b. Anesthesia | [multipurpose_scalpel] 2a. Apply a Multipurpose Scalpel in the Surgical Incision mode | [scalpel] 2b. Apply a Scalpel | [hemostat] 3. Hemostat | [skin_retractors] 4. Skin Retractors | [sutures] 5. Suture to finish
Infotexts.ntg.description.osteosynthesis_surgery: Surgery for bone repair, either for Bone Death (must be done on all limbs in this case) or to repair fractures.
Infotexts.ntg.steps.osteosynthesis_surgery: [analgesia] 1a. Analgesia | [anesthesia] 1b. Anesthesia | [multipurpose_scalpel] 2a. Apply a Multipurpose Scalpel in the Surgical Incision mode | [scalpel] 2b. Apply a Scalpel | [hemostat] 3. Hemostat | [skin_retractors] 4. Skin Retractors | [surgical_drill] 5. Surgical Drill | [osteosynthesis_implants] 6. Osteosynthesis Implants | [sutures] 7. Suture to finish
Infotexts.ntg.steps.aortic_rupture_surgery: [analgesia] 1a. Analgesia | [anesthesia] 1b. Anesthesia | [multipurpose_scalpel] 2a. Apply a Multipurpose Scalpel in the Surgical Incision mode | [scalpel] 2b. Apply a Scalpel | [endovascular_balloon] 3. Endovascular Balloon | [medical_stent] 4. Medical Stent | [sutures] 5. Suture to finish
Infotexts.ntg.description.amputation_surgery: Surgery for limb removal or reattachment. If no limbs were re-attached, it will cause Surgical Amputation
Infotexts.ntg.steps.amputation_surgery: [analgesia] 1a. Analgesia | [anesthesia] 1b. Anesthesia | [multipurpose_scalpel] 2a. Apply a Multipurpose Scalpel in the Surgical Incision mode | [scalpel] 2b. Apply a Scalpel | [hemostat] 3. Hemostat | [skin_retractors] 4. Skin Retractors | [surgical_saw] 5. Surgical Saw | [arm_item] 6. Optional: Reattach a limb (Arm / Leg, Severed / Bionic) | [sutures] 7. Suture to finish
Infotexts.ntg.steps.organ_transplant_surgery: [analgesia] 1a. Analgesia | [anesthesia] 1b. Anesthesia | [multipurpose_scalpel] 2a. Apply a Multipurpose Scalpel in the Surgical Incision mode | [scalpel] 2b. Apply a Scalpel | [hemostat] 3. Hemostat | [skin_retractors] 4. Skin Retractors | [multipurpose_scalpel] 5. Multipurpose Scalpel (set to Adaptive Organ Extraction mode and applied to the right limb as per the description) | 6. Either a new organ (will swap with the one currently in the body) or the Multipurpose Scalpel again (removes the organ in the body without swapping) | [sutures] 7. Suture to finish
